1317: def _aggregate_docstring(based_on=None):
1318:     # Insert common groupby-aggregation docstring.
1319:     # Use `based_on` parameter to add note about the
1320:     # Pandas method the Dask version is based on.
1321:     based_on_str = "\n" if based_on is None else f"\nBased on {based_on}\n"
1322: 
1323:     def wrapper(func):
1324:         func.__doc__ = f"""Aggregate using one or more specified operations
1325:         {based_on_str}
1326:         Parameters
1327:         ----------
1328:         arg : callable, str, list or dict, optional
1329:             Aggregation spec. Accepted combinations are:
1330: 
1331:             - callable function
1332:             - string function name
1333:             - list of functions and/or function names, e.g. ``[np.sum, 'mean']``
1334:             - dict of column names -> function, function name or list of such.
1335:             - None only if named aggregation syntax is used
1336:         split_every : int, optional
1337:             Number of intermediate partitions that may be aggregated at once.
1338:             This defaults to 8. If your intermediate partitions are likely to
1339:             be small (either due to a small number of groups or a small initial
1340:             partition size), consider increasing this number for better performance.
1341:         split_out : int, optional
1342:             Number of output partitions. Default is 1.
1343:         shuffle : bool or str, optional
1344:             Whether a shuffle-based algorithm should be used. A specific
1345:             algorithm name may also be specified (e.g. ``"tasks"`` or ``"p2p"``).
1346:             The shuffle-based algorithm is likely to be more efficient than
1347:             ``shuffle=False`` when ``split_out>1`` and the number of unique
1348:             groups is large (high cardinality). Default is ``False`` when
1349:             ``split_out = 1``. When ``split_out > 1``, it chooses the algorithm
1350:             set by the ``shuffle`` option in the dask config system, or ``"tasks"``
1351:             if nothing is set.
1352:         kwargs: tuple or pd.NamedAgg, optional
1353:             Used for named aggregations where the keywords are the output column
1354:             names and the values are tuples where the first element is the input
1355:             column name and the second element is the aggregation function.
1356:             ``pandas.NamedAgg`` can also be used as the value. To use the named
1357:             aggregation syntax, arg must be set to None.
1358:         """
1359:         return func
1360: 
1361:     return wrapper
1362: 
1363: 
1364: class _GroupBy:
1365:     """Superclass for DataFrameGroupBy and SeriesGroupBy
1366: 
1367:     Parameters
1368:     ----------
1369: 
1370:     obj: DataFrame or Series
1371:         DataFrame or Series to be grouped
1372:     by: str, list or Series
1373:         The key for grouping
1374:     slice: str, list
1375:         The slice keys applied to GroupBy result
1376:     group_keys: bool | None
1377:         Passed to pandas.DataFrame.groupby()
1378:     dropna: bool
1379:         Whether to drop null values from groupby index
1380:     sort: bool
1381:         Passed along to aggregation methods. If allowed,
1382:         the output aggregation will have sorted keys.
1383:     observed: bool, default False
1384:         This only applies if any of the groupers are Categoricals.
1385:         If True: only show observed values for categorical groupers.
1386:         If False: show all values for categorical groupers.
1387:     """
1388: 
1389:     def __init__(
1390:         self,
1391:         df,
1392:         by=None,
1393:         slice=None,
1394:         group_keys=GROUP_KEYS_DEFAULT,
1395:         dropna=None,
1396:         sort=True,
1397:         observed=None,
1398:     ):
1399:         by_ = by if isinstance(by, (tuple, list)) else [by]
1400:         if any(isinstance(key, pd.Grouper) for key in by_):
1401:             raise NotImplementedError("pd.Grouper is currently not supported by Dask.")
1402: 
1403:         # slicing key applied to _GroupBy instance
1404:         self._slice = slice
1405: 
1406:         # Check if we can project columns
1407:         projection = None
1408:         if (
1409:             np.isscalar(self._slice)
1410:             or isinstance(self._slice, (str, list, tuple))
1411:             or (
1412:                 (is_index_like(self._slice) or is_series_like(self._slice))
1413:                 and not is_dask_collection(self._slice)
1414:             )
1415:         ):
1416:             projection = set(by_).union(
1417:                 {self._slice}
1418:                 if (np.isscalar(self._slice) or isinstance(self._slice, str))
1419:                 else self._slice
1420:             )
1421:             projection = [c for c in df.columns if c in projection]
1422: 
1423:         assert isinstance(df, (DataFrame, Series))
1424:         self.group_keys = group_keys
1425:         self.obj = df[projection] if projection else df
1426:         # grouping key passed via groupby method
1427:         self.by = _normalize_by(df, by)
1428:         self.sort = sort
1429: 
1430:         partitions_aligned = all(
1431:             item.npartitions == df.npartitions if isinstance(item, Series) else True
1432:             for item in (self.by if isinstance(self.by, (tuple, list)) else [self.by])
1433:         )
1434: 
1435:         if not partitions_aligned:
1436:             raise NotImplementedError(
1437:                 "The grouped object and 'by' of the groupby must have the same divisions."
1438:             )
1439: 
1440:         if isinstance(self.by, list):
1441:             by_meta = [
1442:                 item._meta if isinstance(item, Series) else item for item in self.by
1443:             ]
1444: 
1445:         elif isinstance(self.by, Series):
1446:             by_meta = self.by._meta
1447:         else:
1448:             by_meta = self.by
1449: 
1450:         self.dropna = {}
1451:         if dropna is not None:
1452:             self.dropna["dropna"] = dropna
1453: 
1454:         # Hold off on setting observed by default: https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/6951
1455:         self.observed = {}
1456:         if observed is not None:
1457:             self.observed["observed"] = observed
1458: 
1459:         # raises a warning about observed=False with pandas>=2.1.
1460:         # We want to raise here, and not later down the stack.
1461:         self._meta = self.obj._meta.groupby(
1462:             by_meta, group_keys=group_keys, **self.observed, **self.dropna
1463:         )
1464: 
1465:     @property
1466:     @_deprecated()
1467:     def index(self):
1468:         return self.by
1469: 
1470:     @index.setter
1471:     def index(self, value):
1472:         self.by = value
1473: 
1474:     @property
1475:     def _groupby_kwargs(self):
1476:         return {
1477:             "by": self.by,
1478:             "group_keys": self.group_keys,
1479:             **self.dropna,
1480:             "sort": self.sort,
1481:             **self.observed,
1482:         }
1483: 
1484:     def __iter__(self):
1485:         raise NotImplementedError(
1486:             "Iteration of DataFrameGroupBy objects requires computing the groups which "
1487:             "may be slow. You probably want to use 'apply' to execute a function for "
1488:             "all the columns. To access individual groups, use 'get_group'. To list "
1489:             "all the group names, use 'df[<group column>].unique().compute()'."
1490:         )
1491: 
1492:     @property
1493:     def _meta_nonempty(self):
1494:         """
1495:         Return a pd.DataFrameGroupBy / pd.SeriesGroupBy which contains sample data.
1496:         """
1497:         sample = self.obj._meta_nonempty
1498: 
1499:         if isinstance(self.by, list):
1500:             by_meta = [
1501:                 item._meta_nonempty if isinstance(item, Series) else item
1502:                 for item in self.by
1503:             ]
1504: 
1505:         elif isinstance(self.by, Series):
1506:             by_meta = self.by._meta_nonempty
1507: 
1508:         else:
1509:             by_meta = self.by
1510: 
1511:         with check_observed_deprecation():
1512:             grouped = sample.groupby(
1513:                 by_meta,
1514:                 group_keys=self.group_keys,
1515:                 **self.observed,
1516:                 **self.dropna,
1517:             )
1518:         return _maybe_slice(grouped, self._slice)
1519: 
1520:     def _single_agg(
1521:         self,
1522:         token,
1523:         func,
1524:         aggfunc=None,
1525:         meta=None,
1526:         split_every=None,
1527:         split_out=1,
1528:         shuffle=None,
1529:         chunk_kwargs=None,
1530:         aggregate_kwargs=None,
1531:         columns=None,
1532:     ):
1533:         """
1534:         Aggregation with a single function/aggfunc rather than a compound spec
1535:         like in GroupBy.aggregate
1536:         """
1537:         shuffle = _determine_shuffle(shuffle, split_out)
1538: 
1539:         if self.sort is None and split_out > 1:
1540:             warnings.warn(SORT_SPLIT_OUT_WARNING, FutureWarning)
1541: 
1542:         if aggfunc is None:
1543:             aggfunc = func
1544: 
1545:         if chunk_kwargs is None:
1546:             chunk_kwargs = {}
1547: 
1548:         if aggregate_kwargs is None:
1549:             aggregate_kwargs = {}
1550: 
1551:         if meta is None:
1552:             with check_numeric_only_deprecation():
1553:                 meta = func(self._meta_nonempty, **chunk_kwargs)
1554: 
1555:         if columns is None:
1556:             columns = meta.name if is_series_like(meta) else meta.columns
1557: 
1558:         args = [self.obj] + (self.by if isinstance(self.by, list) else [self.by])
1559: 
1560:         token = self._token_prefix + token
1561:         levels = _determine_levels(self.by)
1562: 
1563:         if shuffle:
1564:             return _shuffle_aggregate(
1565:                 args,
1566:                 chunk=_apply_chunk,
1567:                 chunk_kwargs={
1568:                     "chunk": func,
1569:                     "columns": columns,
1570:                     **self.observed,
1571:                     **self.dropna,
1572:                     **chunk_kwargs,
1573:                 },
1574:                 aggregate=_groupby_aggregate,
1575:                 aggregate_kwargs={
1576:                     "aggfunc": aggfunc,
1577:                     "levels": levels,
1578:                     **self.observed,
1579:                     **self.dropna,
1580:                     **aggregate_kwargs,
1581:                 },
1582:                 token=token,
1583:                 split_every=split_every,
1584:                 split_out=split_out,
1585:                 shuffle=shuffle,
1586:                 sort=self.sort,
1587:             )
1588: 
1589:         return aca(
1590:             args,
1591:             chunk=_apply_chunk,
1592:             chunk_kwargs=dict(
1593:                 chunk=func,
1594:                 columns=columns,
1595:                 **self.observed,
1596:                 **chunk_kwargs,
1597:                 **self.dropna,
1598:             ),
1599:             aggregate=_groupby_aggregate,
1600:             meta=meta,
1601:             token=token,
1602:             split_every=split_every,
1603:             aggregate_kwargs=dict(
1604:                 aggfunc=aggfunc,
1605:                 levels=levels,
1606:                 **self.observed,
1607:                 **aggregate_kwargs,
1608:                 **self.dropna,
1609:             ),
1610:             split_out=split_out,
1611:             split_out_setup=split_out_on_index,
1612:             sort=self.sort,
1613:         )

1615:     def _cum_agg(self, token, chunk, aggregate, initial):
1616:         """Wrapper for cumulative groupby operation"""
1617:         meta = chunk(self._meta)
1618:         columns = meta.name if is_series_like(meta) else meta.columns
1619:         by_cols = self.by if isinstance(self.by, list) else [self.by]
1620: 
1621:         # rename "by" columns internally
1622:         # to fix cumulative operations on the same "by" columns
1623:         # ref: https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/9313
1624:         if columns is not None:
1625:             # Handle series case (only a single column, but can't
1626:             # enlist above because that fails in pandas when
1627:             # constructing meta later)
1628:             grouping_columns = [columns] if is_series_like(meta) else columns
1629:             to_rename = set(grouping_columns) & set(by_cols)
1630:             by = []
1631:             for col in by_cols:
1632:                 if col in to_rename:
1633:                     suffix = str(uuid.uuid4())
1634:                     self.obj = self.obj.assign(**{col + suffix: self.obj[col]})
1635:                     by.append(col + suffix)
1636:                 else:
1637:                     by.append(col)
1638:         else:
1639:             by = by_cols
1640: 
1641:         name = self._token_prefix + token
1642:         name_part = name + "-map"
1643:         name_last = name + "-take-last"
1644:         name_cum = name + "-cum-last"
1645: 
1646:         # cumulate each partitions
1647:         cumpart_raw = map_partitions(
1648:             _apply_chunk,
1649:             self.obj,
1650:             *by,
1651:             chunk=chunk,
1652:             columns=columns,
1653:             token=name_part,
1654:             meta=meta,
1655:             **self.dropna,
1656:         )
1657: 
1658:         cumpart_raw_frame = (
1659:             cumpart_raw.to_frame() if is_series_like(meta) else cumpart_raw
1660:         )
1661: 
1662:         cumpart_ext = cumpart_raw_frame.assign(
1663:             **{
1664:                 i: self.obj[i]
1665:                 if np.isscalar(i) and i in getattr(self.obj, "columns", [])
1666:                 else self.obj.index
1667:                 for i in by
1668:             }
1669:         )
1670: 
1671:         # Use pd.Grouper objects to specify that we are grouping by columns.
1672:         # Otherwise, pandas will throw an ambiguity warning if the
1673:         # DataFrame's index (self.obj.index) was included in the grouping
1674:         # specification (self.by). See pandas #14432
1675:         grouper = grouper_dispatch(self._meta.obj)
1676:         by_groupers = [grouper(key=ind) for ind in by]
1677:         cumlast = map_partitions(
1678:             _apply_chunk,
1679:             cumpart_ext,
1680:             *by_groupers,
1681:             columns=0 if columns is None else columns,
1682:             chunk=M.last,
1683:             meta=meta,
1684:             token=name_last,
1685:             **self.dropna,
1686:         )
1687: 
1688:         # aggregate cumulated partitions and its previous last element
1689:         _hash = tokenize(self, token, chunk, aggregate, initial)
1690:         name += "-" + _hash
1691:         name_cum += "-" + _hash
1692:         dask = {}
1693:         dask[(name, 0)] = (cumpart_raw._name, 0)
1694: 
1695:         for i in range(1, self.obj.npartitions):
1696:             # store each cumulative step to graph to reduce computation
1697:             if i == 1:
1698:                 dask[(name_cum, i)] = (cumlast._name, i - 1)
1699:             else:
1700:                 # aggregate with previous cumulation results
1701:                 dask[(name_cum, i)] = (
1702:                     _cum_agg_filled,
1703:                     (name_cum, i - 1),
1704:                     (cumlast._name, i - 1),
1705:                     aggregate,
1706:                     initial,
1707:                 )
1708:             dask[(name, i)] = (
1709:                 _cum_agg_aligned,
1710:                 (cumpart_ext._name, i),
1711:                 (name_cum, i),
1712:                 by,
1713:                 0 if columns is None else columns,
1714:                 aggregate,
1715:                 initial,
1716:             )
1717: 
1718:         dependencies = [cumpart_raw]
1719:         if self.obj.npartitions > 1:
1720:             dependencies += [cumpart_ext, cumlast]
1721: 
1722:         graph = HighLevelGraph.from_collections(name, dask, dependencies=dependencies)
1723:         return new_dd_object(graph, name, chunk(self._meta), self.obj.divisions)
1724: 
1725:     def compute(self, **kwargs):
1726:         raise NotImplementedError(
1727:             "DataFrameGroupBy does not allow compute method."
1728:             "Please chain it with an aggregation method (like ``.mean()``) or get a "
1729:             "specific group using ``.get_group()`` before calling ``compute()``"
1730:         )

1732:     def _shuffle(self, meta):
1733:         df = self.obj
1734: 
1735:         if isinstance(self.obj, Series):
1736:             # Temporarily convert series to dataframe for shuffle
1737:             df = df.to_frame("__series__")
1738:             convert_back_to_series = True
1739:         else:
1740:             convert_back_to_series = False
1741: 
1742:         if isinstance(self.by, DataFrame):  # add by columns to dataframe
1743:             df2 = df.assign(**{"_by_" + c: self.by[c] for c in self.by.columns})
1744:         elif isinstance(self.by, Series):
1745:             df2 = df.assign(_by=self.by)
1746:         else:
1747:             df2 = df
1748: 
1749:         df3 = df2.shuffle(on=self.by)  # shuffle dataframe and index
1750: 
1751:         if isinstance(self.by, DataFrame):
1752:             # extract by from dataframe
1753:             cols = ["_by_" + c for c in self.by.columns]
1754:             by2 = df3[cols]
1755:             if is_dataframe_like(meta):
1756:                 df4 = df3.map_partitions(drop_columns, cols, meta.columns.dtype)
1757:             else:
1758:                 df4 = df3.drop(cols, axis=1)
1759:         elif isinstance(self.by, Series):
1760:             by2 = df3["_by"]
1761:             by2.name = self.by.name
1762:             if is_dataframe_like(meta):
1763:                 df4 = df3.map_partitions(drop_columns, "_by", meta.columns.dtype)
1764:             else:
1765:                 df4 = df3.drop("_by", axis=1)
1766:         else:
1767:             df4 = df3
1768:             by2 = self.by
1769: 
1770:         if convert_back_to_series:
1771:             df4 = df4["__series__"].rename(self.obj.name)
1772: 
1773:         return df4, by2

2087:         1. Computing intermediate values for sum, count, and the product of
2088:            all columns: a b c -> a*a, a*b, b*b, b*c, c*c.
2089: 
2090:         2. The values are then aggregated and the final covariance value is calculated:
2091:            cov(X, Y) = X*Y - Xbar * Ybar

2101:         if self._slice:

2103:                 self.obj = self.obj[self._slice]
2104:             else:
2105:                 sliced_plus = list(self._slice) + list(self.by)
2106:                 self.obj = self.obj[sliced_plus]

2108:         result = aca(
2109:             [self.obj, self.by]
2110:             if not isinstance(self.by, list)
2111:             else [self.obj] + self.by,

2116:             aggregate_kwargs={"ddof": ddof, "levels": levels, "std": std},
2117:             combine_kwargs={"levels": levels},
2118:             split_every=split_every,
2119:             split_out=split_out,
2120:             split_out_setup=split_out_on_index,
2121:             sort=self.sort,
2122:         )
2123: 
2124:         if isinstance(self.obj, Series):
2125:             result = result[result.columns[0]]
2126:         if self._slice:
2127:             result = result[self._slice]

2763:             valid observation forward to next valid. bfill: use next valid observation

2769:             to forward/backward fill. In other words, if there is a gap with more than

 107:         )
 108:         presorted = (

 120:     npartitions: Optional[Union[int, Literal["auto"]]] = None,
 121:     ascending: Union[bool, List[bool]] = True,
 122:     na_position: Union[Literal["first"], Literal["last"]] = "last",
 123:     upsample: float = 1.0,

 126:     sort_function_kwargs: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]] = None,
 127:     **kwargs,

 129:     """See DataFrame.sort_values for docstring"""
 130:     if na_position not in ("first", "last"):
 131:         raise ValueError("na_position must be either 'first' or 'last'")

 135:         raise NotImplementedError(

 143:         "ascending": ascending,
 144:         "na_position": na_position,
 145:     }

 147:         sort_function = M.sort_values
 148:     if sort_function_kwargs is not None:
 149:         sort_kwargs.update(sort_function_kwargs)

 151:     if df.npartitions == 1:
 152:         return df.map_partitions(sort_function, **sort_kwargs)
 153: 
 154:     if npartitions == "auto":
 155:         repartition = True

 157:     else:

 167:             isinstance(ascending, list)

 169:             and isinstance(ascending[0], bool)

 172:         else:

 175:             )
 176:

 178:         df, sort_by_col, repartition, npartitions, upsample, partition_size, ascending
 179:     )
 180: 
 181:     if len(divisions) == 2:
 182:         return df.repartition(npartitions=1).map_partitions(
 183:             sort_function, **sort_kwargs
 184:         )
 185: 
 186:     if presorted and npartitions == df.npartitions:
 187:         # divisions are in the right place
 188:         return df.map_partitions(sort_function, **sort_kwargs)
 189: 
 190:     df = rearrange_by_divisions(
 191:         df,
 192:         by[0],
 193:         divisions,
 194:         ascending=ascending,
 195:         na_position=na_position,
 196:         duplicates=False,
 197:     )
 198:     df = df.map_partitions(sort_function, **sort_kwargs)

  49:        functions also take a ``chunks=`` keyword argument.
  50: 
  51:     Parameters
  52:     ----------
  53:     bit_generator : BitGenerator
  54:         BitGenerator to use as the core generator.
  55: 
  56:     Notes
  57:     -----
  58:     The Python stdlib module `random` contains pseudo-random number generator
  59:     with a number of methods that are similar to the ones available in
  60:     ``Generator``. It uses Mersenne Twister, and this bit generator can
  61:     be accessed using ``MT19937``. ``Generator``, besides being
  62:     Dask-aware, has the advantage that it provides a much larger number
  63:     of probability distributions to choose from.
  64: 
  65:     Examples
  66:     --------
  67:     >>> from numpy.random import PCG64
  68:     >>> from dask.array.random import Generator
  69:     >>> rng = Generator(PCG64())
  70:     >>> rng.standard_normal().compute() # doctest: +SKIP
  71:     array(0.44595957)  # random
  72: 
  73:     See Also
  74:     --------
  75:     default_rng : Recommended constructor for `Generator`.
  76:     np.random.Generator
  77:     """
  78: 
  79:     def __init__(self, bit_generator):
  80:         self._bit_generator = bit_generator
  81: 
  82:     def __str__(self):
  83:         _str = self.__class__.__name__
  84:         _str += "(" + self._bit_generator.__class__.__name__ + ")"
  85:         return _str
  86: 
  87:     @property
  88:     def _backend_name(self):
  89:         # Assumes typename(self._RandomState) starts with an
  90:         # array-library name (e.g. "numpy" or "cupy")
  91:         return typename(self._bit_generator).split(".")[0]
  92: 
  93:     @property
  94:     def _backend(self):
  95:         # Assumes `self._backend_name` is an importable
  96:         # array-library name (e.g. "numpy" or "cupy")
  97:         return importlib.import_module(self._backend_name)
  98: 
  99:     @derived_from(np.random.Generator, skipblocks=1)
 100:     def beta(self, a, b, size=None, chunks="auto", **kwargs):
 101:         return _wrap_func(self, "beta", a, b, size=size, chunks=chunks, **kwargs)
 102: 
 103:     @derived_from(np.random.Generator, skipblocks=1)
 104:     def binomial(self, n, p, size=None, chunks="auto", **kwargs):
 105:         return _wrap_func(self, "binomial", n, p, size=size, chunks=chunks, **kwargs)
 106: 
 107:     @derived_from(np.random.Generator, skipblocks=1)
 108:     def chisquare(self, df, size=None, chunks="auto", **kwargs):
 109:         return _wrap_func(self, "chisquare", df, size=size, chunks=chunks, **kwargs)
 110: 
 111:     @derived_from(np.random.Generator, skipblocks=1)
 112:     def choice(
 113:         self,
 114:         a,
 115:         size=None,
 116:         replace=True,
 117:         p=None,
 118:         axis=0,
 119:         shuffle=True,
 120:         chunks="auto",
 121:     ):
 122:         (
 123:             a,
 124:             size,
 125:             replace,
 126:             p,
 127:             axis,
 128:             chunks,
 129:             meta,
 130:             dependencies,
 131:         ) = _choice_validate_params(self, a, size, replace, p, axis, chunks)
 132: 
 133:         sizes = list(product(*chunks))
 134:         bitgens = _spawn_bitgens(self._bit_generator, len(sizes))
 135: 
 136:         name = "da.random.choice-%s" % tokenize(
 137:             bitgens, size, chunks, a, replace, p, axis, shuffle
 138:         )
 139:         keys = product([name], *(range(len(bd)) for bd in chunks))
 140:         dsk = {
 141:             k: (_choice_rng, bitgen, a, size, replace, p, axis, shuffle)
 142:             for k, bitgen, size in zip(keys, bitgens, sizes)
 143:         }
 144: 
 145:         graph = HighLevelGraph.from_collections(name, dsk, dependencies=dependencies)
 146:         return Array(graph, name, chunks, meta=meta)
 147:

 115: class ToParquetFunctionWrapper:
 116:     """
 117:     Parquet Function-Wrapper Class
 118: 
 119:     Writes a DataFrame partition into a distinct parquet
 120:     file. When called, the function also requires the
 121:     current block index (via ``blockwise.BlockIndex``).
 122:     """
 123: 
 124:     def __init__(
 125:         self,
 126:         engine,
 127:         path,
 128:         fs,
 129:         partition_on,
 130:         write_metadata_file,
 131:         i_offset,
 132:         name_function,
 133:         kwargs_pass,
 134:     ):
 135:         self.engine = engine
 136:         self.path = path
 137:         self.fs = fs
 138:         self.partition_on = partition_on
 139:         self.write_metadata_file = write_metadata_file
 140:         self.i_offset = i_offset
 141:         self.name_function = name_function
 142:         self.kwargs_pass = kwargs_pass
 143: 
 144:         # NOTE: __name__ must be with "to-parquet"
 145:         # for the name of the resulting `Blockwise`
 146:         # layer to begin with "to-parquet"
 147:         self.__name__ = "to-parquet"
 148: 
 149:     def __dask_tokenize__(self):
 150:         return (
 151:             self.engine,
 152:             self.path,
 153:             self.fs,
 154:             self.partition_on,
 155:             self.write_metadata_file,
 156:             self.i_offset,
 157:             self.name_function,
 158:             self.kwargs_pass,
 159:         )
 160: 
 161:     def __call__(self, df, block_index: tuple[int]):
 162:         # Get partition index from block index tuple
 163:         part_i = block_index[0]
 164:         filename = (
 165:             f"part.{part_i + self.i_offset}.parquet"
 166:             if self.name_function is None
 167:             else self.name_function(part_i + self.i_offset)
 168:         )
 169: 
 170:         # Write out data
 171:         return self.engine.write_partition(
 172:             df,
 173:             self.path,
 174:             self.fs,
 175:             filename,
 176:             self.partition_on,
 177:             self.write_metadata_file,
 178:             **(dict(self.kwargs_pass, head=True) if part_i == 0 else self.kwargs_pass),
 179:         )
 180: 
 181: 
 182: @dataframe_creation_dispatch.register_inplace("pandas")
 183: def read_parquet(

 196:     blocksize="default",

 200:     **kwargs,"}

content.text:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me create a plan for implementing all the PRs. Given the complexity, I'll implement them in phases. Let me start by creating a comprehensive plan.

The key PRs to implement are:
1. **PR 10159** - Override old default values in `update_defaults` in `dask/config.py`
2. **PR 9936** - Add CLI commands `list` and `get` for dask config in `dask/cli.py`
3. **PR 9947** - Handle string-based engine argument to `read_json` in `dask/dataframe/io/json.py`
4. **PR 10111/10182** - Avoid deprecated `GroupBy.dtypes` and revert grouper-related changes in `dask/dataframe/groupby.py`
5. **PR 10171** - Fix `GroupBy.cov` raising for non-numeric grouping column in `dask/dataframe/groupby.py`
6. **PR 10154** - Updates for Index supporting numpy numeric dtypes in parquet reading
7. **PR 10115** - Preserve dtype for partitioning columns when read with pyarrow
8. **PR 10128** - Handle None column name when checking if columns are all numeric in groupby
9. **PR 10126** - Fix `valid_divisions` when passed a tuple in `dask/dataframe/shuffle.py` (actually it's in utils.py based on grep)
10. **PR 10120** - Maintain annotations in `DataFrame.categorize` in `dask/dataframe/categorical.py`
11. **PR 10042** - Fix handling of missing min/max parquet statistics during filtering in `dask/dataframe/io/parquet/core.py`


12. **PR 10076** - Deprecate `use_nullable_dtypes=` and add `dtype_backend=`
13. **PR 10133** - Deprecate `convert_dtype` in `Series.apply`
14. **PR 10003** - Switch to Generator for random number generation in `dask.array/random.py`
15. **PR 10114** - Make `dask.compatibility` private

Let me implement these systematically. I'll start by focusing on the high-impact PRs that require specific code changes.

task_inputs.impl_update_defaults_fix:
## GOAL
Fix `update_defaults` in dask/config.py to override old default values, as per PR 10159

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `update_defaults` should override values that were previously set as defaults
- Library developers can change Dask's default config values
- A test case verifying the new behavior should work

## INPUTS
- dask/config.py file

## CONSTRAINTS
- Do not change the function signature
- Preserve backward compatibility

## VERIFICATION PLAN
- Run existing tests for config module
- Test the specific case from PR: dask.config.update_defaults({'dataframe': {'backend': 'cudf'}}) should override existing defaults

## OUT OF SCOPE
- Changes to other config functions

## RISKS
- This is a subtle behavioral change, need to verify carefully

## GOAL
Add CLI commands `list` and `get` for dask config, as per PR 9936

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `dask config list` command prints the whole dask config
- `dask config get foo.bar` prints the value of foo.bar
- Commands should integrate with existing CLI infrastructure

## INPUTS
- dask/cli.py file
- dask/config.py file (already imported)

- Follow existing CLI patterns (click-based)

## RISKS
- CLI interface changes need to be backward compatible

## GOAL
Handle string-based engine argument to `read_json` in dask/dataframe/io/json.py, as per PR 9947

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- When a string 'pyarrow' is passed as engine, it should be handled correctly
- Should work with both pyarrow and pandas engines

task_inputs.impl_groupby_dtypes_fix:
## GOAL
Fix GroupBy.dtypes deprecation handling in dask/dataframe/groupby.py, as per PRs 10111 and 10182

- Avoid deprecated GroupBy.dtypes property access
- Revert grouper-related changes that broke dask-cudf compatibility
- Handle None column names when checking if columns are all numeric

## INPUTS
- dask/dataframe/groupby.py
- dask/dataframe/_compat.py

## CONSTRAINTS
- Must work with pandas 2.0+
- Must not break dask-cudf

## VERIFICATION PLAN
- Test groupby operations with various column configurations
- Ensure FutureWarning is not triggered for deprecated dtypes

## OUT OF SCOPE
- Changes to aggregation functions

## RISKS
- Pandas version compatibility
task_inputs.impl_groupby_cov_fix:
## GOAL
Fix GroupBy.cov raising for non-numeric grouping column in dask/dataframe/groupby.py, as per PR 10171

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- GroupBy.cov should not raise for non-numeric grouping columns
- Should handle the case properly without breaking numeric-only operations

## INPUTS
- dask/dataframe/groupby.py

## CONSTRAINTS
- Must work with pandas 2.0+

## VERIFICATION PLAN
- Test cov with non-numeric grouping columns
- Ensure existing cov tests still pass

## OUT OF SCOPE
- Changes to corr function

## RISKS

## GOAL
Fix `valid_divisions` when passed a tuple in dask/dataframe/utils.py, as per PR 10126

- `pd.isnull(tuple)` returns a bool, not a series

- DataFrame.categorize should preserve graph annotations
- Should not drop annotations when computing categories

## INPUTS
- dask/dataframe/categorical.py

## CONSTRAINTS
- Must work with dask.distributed

## VERIFICATION PLAN

- Ensure distributed scheduler compatibility

## OUT OF SCOPE

  80: def categorize(df, columns=None, index=None, split_every=None, **kwargs):
  81:     """Convert columns of the DataFrame to category dtype.
  82: 
  83:     Parameters
  84:     ----------
  85:     columns : list, optional
  86:         A list of column names to convert to categoricals. By default any
  87:         column with an object dtype is converted to a categorical, and any
  88:         unknown categoricals are made known.
  89:     index : bool, optional
  90:         Whether to categorize the index. By default, object indices are
  91:         converted to categorical, and unknown categorical indices are made
  92:         known. Set True to always categorize the index, False to never.
  93:     split_every : int, optional
  94:         Group partitions into groups of this size while performing a
  95:         tree-reduction. If set to False, no tree-reduction will be used.
  96:         Default is 16.
  97:     kwargs
  98:         Keyword arguments are passed on to compute.
  99:     """

 111:     ]

 116:         elif index is None:
 117:             index = str(meta.index.dtype) in ("object", "string")

 120:     if not len(columns) and index is False:

 125:     elif split_every is False:

 127:     elif not isinstance(split_every, Integral) or split_every < 2:

 133:         (a, i): (_get_categories, key, columns, index)
 134:         for (i, key) in enumerate(df.__dask_keys__())
 135:     }
 136:

 138:     k = df.npartitions
 139:     depth = 0

 141:         b = prefix + str(depth)

 144:         k = part_i + 1
 145:         a = b
 146:         depth += 1

 148:     dsk[(prefix, 0)] = (_get_categories_agg, [(a, i) for i in range(k)])
 149:     dsk.update(df.dask)
 150: 
 151:     # Compute the categories
 152:     categories, index = compute_as_if_collection(
 153:         df.__class__, dsk, (prefix, 0), **kwargs
 154:     )

 157:     categories = {k: v.sort_values() for k, v in categories.items()}
 158: 
 159:     # Categorize each partition
 160:     return df.map_partitions(_categorize_block, categories, index)

 163: class CategoricalAccessor(Accessor):
 164:     """
 165:     Accessor object for categorical properties of the Series values.
 166: 
 167:     Examples
 168:     --------
 169:     >>> s.cat.categories  # doctest: +SKIP
 170: 
 171:     Notes
 172:     -----
 173:     Attributes that depend only on metadata are eager
 174: 
 175:     * categories
 176:     * ordered
 177: 
 178:     Attributes depending on the entire dataset are lazy
 179: 
 180:     * codes
 181:     * ...
 182: 
 183:     So `df.a.cat.categories` <=> `df.a._meta.cat.categories`
 184:     So `df.a.cat.codes` <=> `df.a.map_partitions(lambda x: x.cat.codes)`
 185:     """
 186: 
 187:     _accessor_name = "cat"
 188:     _accessor_methods = (
 189:         "add_categories",
 190:         "as_ordered",
 191:         "as_unordered",
 192:         "remove_categories",
 193:         "rename_categories",
 194:         "reorder_categories",
 195:         "set_categories",
 196:     )
 197:     _accessor_properties = ()
 198: 
 199:     @property
 200:     def known(self):
 201:         """Whether the categories are fully known"""
 202:         return has_known_categories(self._series)
 203: 
 204:     def as_known(self, **kwargs):
 205:         """Ensure the categories in this series are known.
 206: 
 207:         If the categories are known, this is a no-op. If unknown, the
 208:         categories are computed, and a new series with known categories is
 209:         returned.
 210: 
 211:         Parameters
 212:         ----------
 213:         kwargs
 214:             Keywords to pass on to the call to `compute`.
 215:         """
 216:         if self.known:
 217:             return self._series
 218:         categories = self._property_map("categories").unique().compute(**kwargs)
 219:         return self.set_categories(categories.values)
 220: 
 221:     def as_unknown(self):
 222:         """Ensure the categories in this series are unknown"""
 223:         if not self.known:
 224:             return self._series
 225:         out = self._series.copy()
 226:         out._meta = clear_known_categories(out._meta)
 227:         return out
 228: 
 229:     @property
 230:     def ordered(self):
 231:         """Whether the categories have an ordered relationship"""
 232:         return self._delegate_property(self._series._meta, "cat", "ordered")
 233: 
 234:     @property
 235:     def categories(self):
 236:         """The categories of this categorical.
 237: 
 238:         If categories are unknown, an error is raised"""
 239:         if not self.known:
 240:             msg = (
 241:                 "`df.column.cat.categories` with unknown categories is not "
 242:                 "supported.  Please use `column.cat.as_known()` or "
 243:                 "`df.categorize()` beforehand to ensure known categories"
 244:             )
 245:             raise AttributeNotImplementedError(msg)
 246:         return self._delegate_property(self._series._meta, "cat", "categories")
 247: 
 248:     @property
 249:     def codes(self):
 250:         """The codes of this categorical.
 251: 
 252:         If categories are unknown, an error is raised"""
 253:         if not self.known:
 254:             msg = (
 255:                 "`df.column.cat.codes` with unknown categories is not "
 256:                 "supported.  Please use `column.cat.as_known()` or "
 257:                 "`df.categorize()` beforehand to ensure known categories"
 258:             )
 259:             raise AttributeNotImplementedError(msg)
 260:         return self._property_map("codes")
 261: 
 262:     def remove_unused_categories(self):
 263:         """
 264:         Removes categories which are not used
 265: 
 266:         Notes
 267:         -----
 268:         This method requires a full scan of the data to compute the
 269:         unique values, which can be expensive.
 270:         """
 271:         # get the set of used categories
 272:         present = self._series.dropna().unique()
 273:         present = pd.Index(present.compute())
 274: 
 275:         if isinstance(self._series._meta, pd.CategoricalIndex):
 276:             meta_cat = self._series._meta
 277:         else:
 278:             meta_cat = self._series._meta.cat
 279: 
 280:         # Reorder to keep cat:code relationship, filtering unused (-1)
 281:         ordered, mask = present.reindex(meta_cat.categories)
 282:         if mask is None:
 283:             # PANDAS-23963: old and new categories match.
 284:             return self._series
 285: 
 286:         new_categories = ordered[mask != -1]
 287:         meta = meta_cat.set_categories(new_categories, ordered=meta_cat.ordered)
 288:         return self._series.map_partitions(
 289:             self._delegate_method,

 291:             "set_categories",
 292:             (),
 293:             {"new_categories": new_categories},
 294:             meta=meta,
 295:             token="cat-set_categories",
 296:         )"}

  52: # #############################################

  54: # GroupBy implementation notes
  55: #
  56: # Dask groupby supports reductions, i.e., mean, sum and alike, and apply. The
  57: # former do not shuffle the data and are efficiently implemented as tree
  58: # reductions. The latter is implemented by shuffling the underlying partitions
  59: # such that all items of a group can be found in the same partition.
  60: #
  61: # The argument to ``.groupby`` (``by``), can be a ``str``, ``dd.DataFrame``,
  62: # ``dd.Series``, or a list thereof. In operations on the grouped object, the
  63: # divisions of the the grouped object and the items of ``by`` have to align.
  64: # Currently, there is no support to shuffle the ``by`` values as part of the
  65: # groupby operation. Therefore, the alignment has to be guaranteed by the
  66: # caller.
  67: #
  68: # To operate on matching partitions, most groupby operations exploit the
  69: # corresponding support in ``apply_concat_apply``. Specifically, this function
  70: # operates on matching partitions of frame-like objects passed as varargs.
  71: #
  72: # After the initial chunk step, ``by``` is implicitly passed along to
  73: # subsequent operations as the index of the partitions. Groupby operations on
  74: # the individual partitions can then access ``by`` via the ``levels``
  75: # parameter of the ``groupby`` function. The correct argument is determined by
  76: # the ``_determine_levels`` function.
  77: #
  78: # To minimize overhead, any ``by`` that is a series contained within the
  79: # dataframe is passed as a columnn key. This transformation is implemented as
  80: # ``_normalize_by``.

  82: # #############################################

 161: def _is_aligned(df, by):
 162:     """Check if ``df`` and ``by`` have aligned indices"""
 163:     if is_series_like(by) or is_dataframe_like(by):
 164:         return df.index.equals(by.index)
 165:     elif isinstance(by, (list, tuple)):
 166:         return all(_is_aligned(df, i) for i in by)
 167:     else:
 168:         return True
 169: 
 170: 
 171: def _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, **kwargs):
 172:     """Groupby, but raise if df and `by` key are unaligned.
 173: 
 174:     Pandas supports grouping by a column that doesn't align with the input
 175:     frame/series/index. However, the reindexing does not seem to be
 176:     threadsafe, and can result in incorrect results. Since grouping by an
 177:     unaligned key is generally a bad idea, we just error loudly in dask.
 178: 
 179:     For more information see pandas GH issue #15244 and Dask GH issue #1876."""
 180:     by = kwargs.get("by", None)
 181:     if by is not None and not _is_aligned(df, by):
 182:         msg = (
 183:             "Grouping by an unaligned column is unsafe and unsupported.\n"
 184:             "This can be caused by filtering only one of the object or\n"
 185:             "grouping key. For example, the following works in pandas,\n"
 186:             "but not in dask:\n"
 187:             "\n"
 188:             "df[df.foo < 0].groupby(df.bar)\n"
 189:             "\n"
 190:             "This can be avoided by either filtering beforehand, or\n"
 191:             "passing in the name of the column instead:\n"
 192:             "\n"
 193:             "df2 = df[df.foo < 0]\n"
 194:             "df2.groupby(df2.bar)\n"
 195:             "# or\n"
 196:             "df[df.foo < 0].groupby('bar')\n"
 197:             "\n"
 198:             "For more information see dask GH issue #1876."
 199:         )
 200:         raise ValueError(msg)
 201:     elif by is not None and len(by):
 202:         # since we're coming through apply, `by` will be a tuple.
 203:         # Pandas treats tuples as a single key, and lists as multiple keys
 204:         # We want multiple keys
 205:         if isinstance(by, str):
 206:             by = [by]
 207:         kwargs.update(by=list(by))
 208:     with check_observed_deprecation():
 209:         return df.groupby(**kwargs)
 210: 
 211: 
 212: def _groupby_slice_apply(
 213:     df,
 214:     grouper,
 215:     key,
 216:     func,
 217:     *args,
 218:     group_keys=GROUP_KEYS_DEFAULT,
 219:     dropna=None,
 220:     observed=None,
 221:     **kwargs,
 222: ):
 223:     # No need to use raise if unaligned here - this is only called after
 224:     # shuffling, which makes everything aligned already
 225:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 226:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 227:     g = df.groupby(grouper, group_keys=group_keys, **observed, **dropna)
 228:     if key:
 229:         g = g[key]
 230:     return g.apply(func, *args, **kwargs)
 231: 
 232: 
 233: def _groupby_slice_transform(
 234:     df,
 235:     grouper,
 236:     key,
 237:     func,
 238:     *args,
 239:     group_keys=GROUP_KEYS_DEFAULT,
 240:     dropna=None,
 241:     observed=None,
 242:     **kwargs,
 243: ):
 244:     # No need to use raise if unaligned here - this is only called after
 245:     # shuffling, which makes everything aligned already
 246:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 247:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 248:     g = df.groupby(grouper, group_keys=group_keys, **observed, **dropna)
 249:     if key:
 250:         g = g[key]
 251: 
 252:     # Cannot call transform on an empty dataframe
 253:     if len(df) == 0:
 254:         return g.apply(func, *args, **kwargs)
 255: 
 256:     return g.transform(func, *args, **kwargs)
 257: 
 258: 
 259: def _groupby_slice_shift(
 260:     df,
 261:     grouper,
 262:     key,
 263:     shuffled,
 264:     group_keys=GROUP_KEYS_DEFAULT,
 265:     dropna=None,
 266:     observed=None,
 267:     **kwargs,
 268: ):
 269:     # No need to use raise if unaligned here - this is only called after
 270:     # shuffling, which makes everything aligned already
 271:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 272:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 273:     if shuffled:
 274:         df = df.sort_index()
 275:     g = df.groupby(grouper, group_keys=group_keys, **observed, **dropna)
 276:     if key:
 277:         g = g[key]
 278:     with check_groupby_axis_deprecation():
 279:         result = g.shift(**kwargs)
 280:     return result
 281: 
 282: 
 283: def _groupby_get_group(df, by_key, get_key, columns):
 284:     # SeriesGroupBy may pass df which includes group key
 285:     grouped = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by_key)
 286: 
 287:     try:
 288:         if is_dataframe_like(df):
 289:             grouped = grouped[columns]
 290:         return grouped.get_group(get_key)
 291:     except KeyError:
 292:         # to create empty DataFrame/Series, which has the same
 293:         # dtype as the original
 294:         if is_dataframe_like(df):
 295:             # may be SeriesGroupBy
 296:             df = df[columns]
 297:         return df.iloc[0:0]
 298:

 300: def numeric_only_deprecate_default(func):
 301:     """Decorator for methods that should warn when numeric_only is default"""
 302: 
 303:     @wraps(func)
 304:     def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
 305:         if isinstance(self, DataFrameGroupBy):
 306:             numeric_only = kwargs.get("numeric_only", no_default)
 307:             # Prior to `pandas=1.5`, `numeric_only` support wasn't uniformly supported
 308:             # in pandas. We don't support `numeric_only=False` in this case.
 309:             if not PANDAS_GT_150 and numeric_only is not no_default:
 310:                 raise NotImplementedError(
 311:                     "'numeric_only=False' is not implemented in Dask."
 312:                 )
 313:             numerics = self.obj._meta._get_numeric_data()
 314:             has_non_numerics = set(self._meta.dtypes.columns) - set(numerics.columns)
 315:             if has_non_numerics and PANDAS_GT_150 and not PANDAS_GT_200:
 316:                 if numeric_only is no_default:
 317:                     warnings.warn(
 318:                         "The default value of numeric_only will be changed to False in "
 319:                         "the future when using dask with pandas 2.0",
 320:                         FutureWarning,
 321:                     )
 322:                 elif numeric_only is False and funcname(func) in ("sum", "prod"):
 323:                     warnings.warn(
 324:                         "Dropping invalid columns is deprecated. In a future version, a TypeError will be raised. "
 325:                         f"Before calling .{funcname(func)}, select only columns which should be valid for the function",
 326:                         FutureWarning,
 327:                     )
 328: 
 329:         return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
 330: 
 331:     return wrapper
 332: 
 333: 
 334: def numeric_only_not_implemented(func):
 335:     """Decorator for methods that can't handle numeric_only=False"""
 336: 
 337:     @wraps(func)
 338:     def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
 339:         if isinstance(self, DataFrameGroupBy):
 340:             maybe_raise = not (
 341:                 func.__name__ == "agg"
 342:                 and len(args) > 0
 343:                 and args[0] not in NUMERIC_ONLY_NOT_IMPLEMENTED
 344:             )
 345:             if maybe_raise:
 346:                 numeric_only = kwargs.get("numeric_only", no_default)
 347:                 # Prior to `pandas=1.5`, `numeric_only` support wasn't uniformly supported
 348:                 # in pandas. We don't support `numeric_only=False` in this case.
 349:                 if not PANDAS_GT_150 and numeric_only is not no_default:
 350:                     raise NotImplementedError(
 351:                         "'numeric_only=False' is not implemented in Dask."
 352:                     )
 353:                 numerics = self.obj._meta._get_numeric_data()
 354:                 has_non_numerics = set(self._meta.dtypes.columns) - set(
 355:                     numerics.columns
 356:                 )
 357:                 if has_non_numerics:
 358:                     if numeric_only is False or (
 359:                         PANDAS_GT_200 and numeric_only is no_default
 360:                     ):
 361:                         raise NotImplementedError(
 362:                             "'numeric_only=False' is not implemented in Dask."
 363:                         )
 364:                     if (
 365:                         PANDAS_GT_150
 366:                         and not PANDAS_GT_200
 367:                         and numeric_only is no_default
 368:                     ):
 369:                         warnings.warn(
 370:                             "The default value of numeric_only will be changed to False "
 371:                             "in the future when using dask with pandas 2.0",
 372:                             FutureWarning,
 373:                         )
 374:         return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
 375: 
 376:     return wrapper
 377:

 379: ###############################################################
 380: # Aggregation
 381: ###############################################################
 382: 
 383: 
 384: class Aggregation:
 385:     """User defined groupby-aggregation.
 386: 
 387:     This class allows users to define their own custom aggregation in terms of
 388:     operations on Pandas dataframes in a map-reduce style. You need to specify
 389:     what operation to do on each chunk of data, how to combine those chunks of
 390:     data together, and then how to finalize the result.
 391: 
 392:     See :ref:`dataframe.groupby.aggregate` for more.
 393: 
 394:     Parameters
 395:     ----------
 396:     name : str
 397:         the name of the aggregation. It should be unique, since intermediate
 398:         result will be identified by this name.
 399:     chunk : callable
 400:         a function that will be called with the grouped column of each
 401:         partition. It can either return a single series or a tuple of series.
 402:         The index has to be equal to the groups.
 403:     agg : callable
 404:         a function that will be called to aggregate the results of each chunk.
 405:         Again the argument(s) will be grouped series. If ``chunk`` returned a
 406:         tuple, ``agg`` will be called with all of them as individual positional
 407:         arguments.
 408:     finalize : callable
 409:         an optional finalizer that will be called with the results from the
 410:         aggregation.
 411: 
 412:     Examples
 413:     --------
 414:     We could implement ``sum`` as follows:
 415: 
 416:     >>> custom_sum = dd.Aggregation(
 417:     ...     name='custom_sum',
 418:     ...     chunk=lambda s: s.sum(),
 419:     ...     agg=lambda s0: s0.sum()
 420:     ... )  # doctest: +SKIP
 421:     >>> df.groupby('g').agg(custom_sum)  # doctest: +SKIP
 422: 
 423:     We can implement ``mean`` as follows:
 424: 
 425:     >>> custom_mean = dd.Aggregation(
 426:     ...     name='custom_mean',
 427:     ...     chunk=lambda s: (s.count(), s.sum()),
 428:     ...     agg=lambda count, sum: (count.sum(), sum.sum()),
 429:     ...     finalize=lambda count, sum: sum / count,
 430:     ... )  # doctest: +SKIP
 431:     >>> df.groupby('g').agg(custom_mean)  # doctest: +SKIP
 432: 
 433:     Though of course, both of these are built-in and so you don't need to
 434:     implement them yourself.
 435:     """
 436: 
 437:     def __init__(self, name, chunk, agg, finalize=None):
 438:         self.chunk = chunk
 439:         self.agg = agg
 440:         self.finalize = finalize
 441:         self.__name__ = name
 442: 
 443: 
 444: def _groupby_aggregate(
 445:     df, aggfunc=None, levels=None, dropna=None, sort=False, observed=None, **kwargs
 446: ):
 447:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 448:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 449: 
 450:     with check_observed_deprecation():
 451:         grouped = df.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort, **observed, **dropna)
 452: 
 453:     # we emit a warning earlier in stack about default numeric_only being deprecated,
 454:     # so there's no need to propagate the warning that pandas emits as well
 455:     with check_numeric_only_deprecation():
 456:         return aggfunc(grouped, **kwargs)
 457: 
 458: 
 459: def _groupby_aggregate_spec(
 460:     df, spec, levels=None, dropna=None, sort=False, observed=None, **kwargs
 461: ):
 462:     """
 463:     A simpler version of _groupby_aggregate that just calls ``aggregate`` using
 464:     the user-provided spec.
 465:     """
 466:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 467:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 468:     return df.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort, **observed, **dropna).aggregate(
 469:         spec, **kwargs
 470:     )
 471: 
 472: 
 473: def _non_agg_chunk(df, *by, key, dropna=None, observed=None, **kwargs):
 474:     """
 475:     A non-aggregation agg function. This simuates the behavior of an initial
 476:     partitionwise aggregation, but doesn't actually aggregate or throw away
 477:     any data.
 478:     """
 479:     if is_series_like(df):
 480:         # Handle a series-like groupby. `by` could be columns that are not the series,
 481:         # but are like-indexed, so we handle that case by temporarily converting to
 482:         # a dataframe, then setting the index.
 483:         result = df.to_frame().set_index(by[0] if len(by) == 1 else list(by))[df.name]
 484:     else:
 485:         # Handle a frame-like groupby.
 486:         result = df.set_index(list(by))
 487:         if isinstance(key, (tuple, list, set, pd.Index)):
 488:             key = list(key)
 489:         result = result[key]
 490: 
 491:     # If observed is False, we have to check for categorical indices and possibly enrich
 492:     # them with unobserved values. This function is intended as an initial partitionwise
 493:     # aggregation, so you might expect that we could enrich the frame with unobserved
 494:     # values at the end. However, if you have multiple output partitions, that results
 495:     # in duplicated unobserved values in each partition. So we have to do this step
 496:     # at the start before any shuffling occurs so that we can consolidate all of the
 497:     # unobserved values in a single partition.
 498:     if observed is False:
 499:         has_categoricals = False
 500: 
 501:         # Search for categorical indices and get new index objects that have all the
 502:         # categories in them.
 503:         if isinstance(result.index, pd.CategoricalIndex):
 504:             has_categoricals = True
 505:             full_index = result.index.categories.copy().rename(result.index.name)
 506:         elif isinstance(result.index, pd.MultiIndex) and any(
 507:             isinstance(level, pd.CategoricalIndex) for level in result.index.levels
 508:         ):
 509:             has_categoricals = True
 510:             full_index = pd.MultiIndex.from_product(
 511:                 (
 512:                     level.categories
 513:                     if isinstance(level, pd.CategoricalIndex)
 514:                     else level
 515:                     for level in result.index.levels
 516:                 ),
 517:                 names=result.index.names,
 518:             )
 519:         if has_categoricals:
 520:             # If we found any categoricals, append unobserved values to the end of the
 521:             # frame.
 522:             new_cats = full_index[~full_index.isin(result.index)]
 523:             empty = pd.DataFrame(pd.NA, index=new_cats, columns=result.columns)
 524:             result = pd.concat([result, empty])
 525: 
 526:     return result
 527: 
 528: 
 529: def _apply_chunk(df, *by, dropna=None, observed=None, **kwargs):
 530:     func = kwargs.pop("chunk")
 531:     columns = kwargs.pop("columns")
 532:     dropna = {"dropna": dropna} if dropna is not None else {}
 533:     observed = {"observed": observed} if observed is not None else {}
 534: 
 535:     g = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by, **observed, **dropna)
 536:     if is_series_like(df) or columns is None:
 537:         return func(g, **kwargs)
 538:     else:
 539:         if isinstance(columns, (tuple, list, set, pd.Index)):
 540:             columns = list(columns)
 541:         return func(g[columns], **kwargs)
 542: 
 543: 
 544: def _var_chunk(df, *by):
 545:     if is_series_like(df):
 546:         df = df.to_frame()
 547: 
 548:     df = df.copy()
 549: 
 550:     g = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by)
 551:     with check_numeric_only_deprecation():
 552:         x = g.sum()
 553: 
 554:     n = g[x.columns].count().rename(columns=lambda c: (c, "-count"))
 555: 
 556:     cols = x.columns
 557:     df[cols] = df[cols] ** 2
 558: 
 559:     g2 = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by)
 560:     with check_numeric_only_deprecation():
 561:         x2 = g2.sum().rename(columns=lambda c: (c, "-x2"))
 562: 
 563:     return concat([x, x2, n], axis=1)
 564: 
 565: 
 566: def _var_combine(g, levels, sort=False):
 567:     return g.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort).sum()
 568: 
 569: 
 570: def _var_agg(g, levels, ddof, sort=False):
 571:     g = g.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort).sum()
 572:     nc = len(g.columns)
 573:     x = g[g.columns[: nc // 3]]
 574:     # chunks columns are tuples (value, name), so we just keep the value part
 575:     x2 = g[g.columns[nc // 3 : 2 * nc // 3]].rename(columns=lambda c: c[0])
 576:     n = g[g.columns[-nc // 3 :]].rename(columns=lambda c: c[0])
 577: 
 578:     # TODO: replace with _finalize_var?
 579:     result = x2 - x**2 / n
 580:     div = n - ddof
 581:     div[div < 0] = 0
 582:     result /= div
 583:     result[(n - ddof) == 0] = np.nan
 584:     assert is_dataframe_like(result)
 585:     result[result < 0] = 0  # avoid rounding errors that take us to zero
 586:     return result
 587: 
 588: 
 589: def _cov_combine(g, levels):
 590:     return g
 591: 
 592: 
 593: def _cov_finalizer(df, cols, std=False):
 594:     vals = []
 595:     num_elements = len(list(it.product(cols, repeat=2)))
 596:     num_cols = len(cols)
 597:     vals = list(range(num_elements))
 598:     col_idx_mapping = dict(zip(cols, range(num_cols)))
 599:     for i, j in it.combinations_with_replacement(df[cols].columns, 2):
 600:         x = col_idx_mapping[i]
 601:         y = col_idx_mapping[j]
 602:         idx = x + num_cols * y
 603:         mul_col = f"{i}{j}"
 604:         ni = df["%s-count" % i]
 605:         nj = df["%s-count" % j]
 606: 
 607:         n = np.sqrt(ni * nj)
 608:         div = n - 1
 609:         div[div < 0] = 0
 610:         val = (df[mul_col] - df[i] * df[j] / n).values[0] / div.values[0]
 611:         if std:
 612:             ii = f"{i}{i}"
 613:             jj = f"{j}{j}"
 614:             std_val_i = (df[ii] - (df[i] ** 2) / ni).values[0] / div.values[0]
 615:             std_val_j = (df[jj] - (df[j] ** 2) / nj).values[0] / div.values[0]
 616:             val = val / np.sqrt(std_val_i * std_val_j)
 617: 
 618:         vals[idx] = val
 619:         if i != j:
 620:             idx = num_cols * x + y
 621:             vals[idx] = val
 622: 
 623:     level_1 = cols
 624:     index = pd.MultiIndex.from_product([level_1, level_1])
 625:     return pd.Series(vals, index=index)
 626: 
 627: 
 628: def _mul_cols(df, cols):
 629:     """Internal function to be used with apply to multiply
 630:     each column in a dataframe by every other column
 631: 
 632:     a b c -> a*a, a*b, b*b, b*c, c*c
 633:     """
 634:     _df = df.__class__()
 635:     for i, j in it.combinations_with_replacement(cols, 2):
 636:         col = f"{i}{j}"
 637:         _df[col] = df[i] * df[j]
 638: 
 639:     # Fix index in a groupby().apply() context
 640:     # https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/8137
 641:     # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/43568
 642:     # Make sure index dtype is int (even if _df is empty)
 643:     # https://github.com/dask/dask/pull/9701
 644:     _df.index = np.zeros(len(_df), dtype=int)
 645:     return _df
 646: 
 647: 
 648: def _cov_chunk(df, *by):
 649:     """Covariance Chunk Logic
 650: 
 651:     Parameters
 652:     ----------
 653:     df : Pandas.DataFrame
 654:     std : bool, optional
 655:         When std=True we are calculating with Correlation
 656: 
 657:     Returns
 658:     -------
 659:     tuple
 660:         Processed X, Multiplied Cols,
 661:     """
 662:     if is_series_like(df):
 663:         df = df.to_frame()
 664:     df = df.copy()
 665: 
 666:     # mapping columns to str(numerical) values allows us to easily handle
 667:     # arbitrary column names (numbers, string, empty strings)
 668:     col_mapping = collections.OrderedDict()
 669:     for i, c in enumerate(df.columns):
 670:         col_mapping[c] = str(i)
 671:     df = df.rename(columns=col_mapping)
 672:     cols = df._get_numeric_data().columns
 673: 
 674:     # when grouping by external series don't exclude columns
 675:     is_mask = any(is_series_like(s) for s in by)
 676:     if not is_mask:
 677:         by = [col_mapping[k] for k in by]
 678:         cols = cols.drop(np.array(by))
 679: 
 680:     g = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by)
 681:     x = g.sum()
 682: 
 683:     mul = g.apply(_mul_cols, cols=cols).reset_index(level=-1, drop=True)
 684: 
 685:     n = g[x.columns].count().rename(columns=lambda c: f"{c}-count")
 686:     return (x, mul, n, col_mapping)
 687: 
 688: 
 689: def _cov_agg(_t, levels, ddof, std=False, sort=False):
 690:     sums = []
 691:     muls = []
 692:     counts = []
 693: 
 694:     # sometime we get a series back from concat combiner
 695:     t = list(_t)
 696: 
 697:     cols = t[0][0].columns
 698:     for x, mul, n, col_mapping in t:
 699:         sums.append(x)
 700:         muls.append(mul)
 701:         counts.append(n)
 702:         col_mapping = col_mapping
 703: 
 704:     total_sums = concat(sums).groupby(level=levels, sort=sort).sum()
 705:     total_muls = concat(muls).groupby(level=levels, sort=sort).sum()
 706:     total_counts = concat(counts).groupby(level=levels).sum()
 707:     result = (
 708:         concat([total_sums, total_muls, total_counts], axis=1)
 709:         .groupby(level=levels)
 710:         .apply(_cov_finalizer, cols=cols, std=std)
 711:     )
 712: 
 713:     inv_col_mapping = {v: k for k, v in col_mapping.items()}
 714:     idx_vals = result.index.names
 715:     idx_mapping = list()
 716: 
 717:     # when index is None we probably have selected a particular column
 718:     # df.groupby('a')[['b']].cov()
 719:     if len(idx_vals) == 1 and all(n is None for n in idx_vals):
 720:         idx_vals = list(inv_col_mapping.keys() - set(total_sums.columns))
 721: 
 722:     for val in idx_vals:
 723:         idx_name = inv_col_mapping.get(val, val)
 724:         idx_mapping.append(idx_name)
 725: 
 726:         if len(result.columns.levels[0]) < len(col_mapping):
 727:             # removing index from col_mapping (produces incorrect multiindexes)
 728:             try:
 729:                 col_mapping.pop(idx_name)
 730:             except KeyError:
 731:                 # when slicing the col_map will not have the index
 732:                 pass
 733: 
 734:     keys = list(col_mapping.keys())
 735:     for level in range(len(result.columns.levels)):
 736:         result.columns = result.columns.set_levels(keys, level=level)
 737: 
 738:     result.index.set_names(idx_mapping, inplace=True)
 739: 
 740:     # stacking can lead to a sorted index
 741:     s_result = result.stack(dropna=False)
 742:     assert is_dataframe_like(s_result)
 743:     return s_result
 744: 
 745: 
 746: ###############################################################
 747: # nunique
 748: ###############################################################
 749: def _drop_duplicates_reindex(df):
 750:     # Fix index in a groupby().apply() context
 751:     # https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/8137
 752:     # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/43568
 753:     # Make sure index dtype is int (even if result is empty)
 754:     # https://github.com/dask/dask/pull/9701
 755:     result = df.drop_duplicates()
 756:     result.index = np.zeros(len(result), dtype=int)
 757:     return result
 758: 
 759: 
 760: def _nunique_df_chunk(df, *by, **kwargs):
 761:     name = kwargs.pop("name")
 762: 
 763:     g = _groupby_raise_unaligned(df, by=by)
 764:     if len(df) > 0:
 765:         grouped = (
 766:             g[[name]].apply(_drop_duplicates_reindex).reset_index(level=-1, drop=True)
 767:         )
 768:     else:
 769:         # Manually create empty version, since groupby-apply for empty frame
 770:         # results in df with no columns
 771:         grouped = g[[name]].nunique()
 772:         grouped = grouped.astype(df.dtypes[grouped.columns].to_dict())
 773: 
 774:     return grouped
 775: 
 776: 
 777: def _nunique_df_combine(df, levels, sort=False):
 778:     result = (
 779:         df.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort)
 780:         .apply(_drop_duplicates_reindex)
 781:         .reset_index(level=-1, drop=True)
 782:     )
 783:     return result
 784: 
 785: 
 786: def _nunique_df_aggregate(df, levels, name, sort=False):
 787:     with check_observed_deprecation():
 788:         return df.groupby(level=levels, sort=sort)[name].nunique()
 789: 
 790: 
 791: def _nunique_series_chunk(df, *by, **_ignored_):
 792:     # convert series to data frame, then hand over to dataframe code path
 793:     assert is_series_like(df)
 794: 
 795:     df = df.to_frame()
 796:     kwargs = dict(name=df.columns[0], levels=_determine_levels(by))
 797:     return _nunique_df_chunk(df, *by, **kwargs)
 798:
