 115: class ToParquetFunctionWrapper:
 116:     """
 117:     Parquet Function-Wrapper Class
 118: 
 119:     Writes a DataFrame partition into a distinct parquet
 120:     file. When called, the function also requires the
 121:     current block index (via ``blockwise.BlockIndex``).
 122:     """
 123: 
 124:     def __init__(
 125:         self,
 126:         engine,
 127:         path,
 128:         fs,
 129:         partition_on,
 130:         write_metadata_file,
 131:         i_offset,
 132:         name_function,
 133:         kwargs_pass,
 134:     ):
 135:         self.engine = engine
 136:         self.path = path
 137:         self.fs = fs
 138:         self.partition_on = partition_on
 139:         self.write_metadata_file = write_metadata_file
 140:         self.i_offset = i_offset
 141:         self.name_function = name_function
 142:         self.kwargs_pass = kwargs_pass
 143: 
 144:         # NOTE: __name__ must be with "to-parquet"
 145:         # for the name of the resulting `Blockwise`
 146:         # layer to begin with "to-parquet"
 147:         self.__name__ = "to-parquet"
 148: 
 149:     def __dask_tokenize__(self):
 150:         return (
 151:             self.engine,
 152:             self.path,
 153:             self.fs,
 154:             self.partition_on,
 155:             self.write_metadata_file,
 156:             self.i_offset,
 157:             self.name_function,
 158:             self.kwargs_pass,
 159:         )
 160: 
 161:     def __call__(self, df, block_index: tuple[int]):
 162:         # Get partition index from block index tuple
 163:         part_i = block_index[0]
 164:         filename = (
 165:             f"part.{part_i + self.i_offset}.parquet"
 166:             if self.name_function is None
 167:             else self.name_function(part_i + self.i_offset)
 168:         )
 169: 
 170:         # Write out data
 171:         return self.engine.write_partition(
 172:             df,
 173:             self.path,
 174:             self.fs,
 175:             filename,
 176:             self.partition_on,
 177:             self.write_metadata_file,
 178:             **(dict(self.kwargs_pass, head=True) if part_i == 0 else self.kwargs_pass),
 179:         )
 180: 
 181: 
 182: @dataframe_creation_dispatch.register_inplace("pandas")
 183: def read_parquet(
 184:     path,
 185:     columns=None,
 186:     filters=None,
 187:     categories=None,
 188:     index=None,
 189:     storage_options=None,
 190:     engine="auto",
 191:     use_nullable_dtypes: bool = False,
 192:     calculate_divisions=None,
 193:     ignore_metadata_file=False,
 194:     metadata_task_size=None,
 195:     split_row_groups="infer",
 196:     blocksize="default",
 197:     aggregate_files=None,
 198:     parquet_file_extension=(".parq", ".parquet", ".pq"),
 199:     filesystem=None,
 200:     **kwargs,
 201: ):
 202:     """
 203:     Read a Parquet file into a Dask DataFrame
 204: 
 205:     This reads a directory of Parquet data into a Dask.dataframe, one file per
 206:     partition.  It selects the index among the sorted columns if any exist.
 207: 
 208:     Parameters
 209:     ----------
 210:     path : str or list
 211:         Source directory for data, or path(s) to individual parquet files.
 212:         Prefix with a protocol like ``s3://`` to read from alternative
 213:         filesystems. To read from multiple files you can pass a globstring or a
 214:         list of paths, with the caveat that they must all have the same
 215:         protocol.
 216:     columns : str or list, default None
 217:         Field name(s) to read in as columns in the output. By default all
 218:         non-index fields will be read (as determined by the pandas parquet
 219:         metadata, if present). Provide a single field name instead of a list to
 220:         read in the data as a Series.
 221:     filters : Union[List[Tuple[str, str, Any]], List[List[Tuple[str, str, Any]]]], default None
 222:         List of filters to apply, like ``[[('col1', '==', 0), ...], ...]``.
 223:         Using this argument will NOT result in row-wise filtering of the final
 224:         partitions unless ``engine="pyarrow"`` is also specified.  For
 225:         other engines, filtering is only performed at the partition level, that is,
 226:         to prevent the loading of some row-groups and/or files.
 227: 
 228:         For the "pyarrow" engine, predicates can be expressed in disjunctive
 229:         normal form (DNF). This means that the inner-most tuple describes a single
 230:         column predicate. These inner predicates are combined with an AND
 231:         conjunction into a larger predicate. The outer-most list then combines all
 232:         of the combined filters with an OR disjunction.
 233: 
 234:         Predicates can also be expressed as a ``List[Tuple]``. These are evaluated
 235:         as an AND conjunction. To express OR in predicates, one must use the
 236:         (preferred for "pyarrow") ``List[List[Tuple]]`` notation.
 237: 
 238:         Note that the "fastparquet" engine does not currently support DNF for
 239:         the filtering of partitioned columns (``List[Tuple]`` is required).
 240:     index : str, list or False, default None
 241:         Field name(s) to use as the output frame index. By default will be
 242:         inferred from the pandas parquet file metadata, if present. Use ``False``
 243:         to read all fields as columns.
 244:     categories : list or dict, default None
 245:         For any fields listed here, if the parquet encoding is Dictionary,
 246:         the column will be created with dtype category. Use only if it is
 247:         guaranteed that the column is encoded as dictionary in all row-groups.
 248:         If a list, assumes up to 2**16-1 labels; if a dict, specify the number
 249:         of labels expected; if None, will load categories automatically for
 250:         data written by dask/fastparquet, not otherwise.
 251:     storage_options : dict, default None
 252:         Key/value pairs to be passed on to the file-system backend, if any.
 253:         Note that the default file-system backend can be configured with the
 254:         ``filesystem`` argument, described below.
 255:     open_file_options : dict, default None
 256:         Key/value arguments to be passed along to ``AbstractFileSystem.open``
 257:         when each parquet data file is open for reading. Experimental
 258:         (optimized) "precaching" for remote file systems (e.g. S3, GCS) can
 259:         be enabled by adding ``{"method": "parquet"}`` under the
 260:         ``"precache_options"`` key. Also, a custom file-open function can be
 261:         used (instead of ``AbstractFileSystem.open``), by specifying the
 262:         desired function under the ``"open_file_func"`` key.
 263:     engine : {'auto', 'pyarrow', 'fastparquet'}, default 'auto'
 264:         Parquet library to use. Defaults to 'auto', which uses ``pyarrow`` if
 265:         it is installed, and falls back to ``fastparquet`` otherwise.
 266:     use_nullable_dtypes : {False, True}
 267:         Whether to use extension dtypes for the resulting ``DataFrame``.
 268:         ``use_nullable_dtypes=True`` is only supported when ``engine="pyarrow"``.
 269: 
 270:         .. note::
 271: 
 272:             Use the ``dataframe.dtype_backend`` config option to select which
 273:             dtype implementation to use.
 274: 
 275:             ``dataframe.dtype_backend="pandas"`` (the default) will use
 276:             pandas' ``numpy``-backed nullable dtypes (e.g. ``Int64``,
 277:             ``string[python]``, etc.) while ``dataframe.dtype_backend="pyarrow"``
 278:             will use ``pyarrow``-backed extension dtypes (e.g. ``int64[pyarrow]``,
 279:             ``string[pyarrow]``, etc.). ``dataframe.dtype_backend="pyarrow"``
 280:             requires ``pandas`` 1.5+.
 281: 
 282:     calculate_divisions : bool, default False
 283:         Whether to use min/max statistics from the footer metadata (or global
 284:         ``_metadata`` file) to calculate divisions for the output DataFrame
 285:         collection. Divisions will not be calculated if statistics are missing.
 286:         This option will be ignored if ``index`` is not specified and there is
 287:         no physical index column specified in the custom "pandas" Parquet
 288:         metadata. Note that ``calculate_divisions=True`` may be extremely slow
 289:         when no global ``_metadata`` file is present, especially when reading
 290:         from remote storage. Set this to ``True`` only when known divisions
 291:         are needed for your workload (see :ref:`dataframe-design-partitions`).
 292:     ignore_metadata_file : bool, default False
 293:         Whether to ignore the global ``_metadata`` file (when one is present).
 294:         If ``True``, or if the global ``_metadata`` file is missing, the parquet
 295:         metadata may be gathered and processed in parallel. Parallel metadata
 296:         processing is currently supported for ``ArrowDatasetEngine`` only.
 297:     metadata_task_size : int, default configurable
 298:         If parquet metadata is processed in parallel (see ``ignore_metadata_file``
 299:         description above), this argument can be used to specify the number of
 300:         dataset files to be processed by each task in the Dask graph.  If this
 301:         argument is set to ``0``, parallel metadata processing will be disabled.
 302:         The default values for local and remote filesystems can be specified
 303:         with the "metadata-task-size-local" and "metadata-task-size-remote"
 304:         config fields, respectively (see "dataframe.parquet").
 305:     split_row_groups : 'infer', 'adaptive', bool, or int, default 'infer'
 306:         If True, then each output dataframe partition will correspond to a single
 307:         parquet-file row-group. If False, each partition will correspond to a
 308:         complete file.  If a positive integer value is given, each dataframe
 309:         partition will correspond to that number of parquet row-groups (or fewer).
 310:         If 'adaptive', the metadata of each file will be used to ensure that every
 311:         partition satisfies ``blocksize``. If 'infer' (the default), the
 312:         uncompressed storage-size metadata in the first file will be used to
 313:         automatically set ``split_row_groups`` to either 'adaptive' or ``False``.
 314:     blocksize : int or str, default 'default'
 315:         The desired size of each output ``DataFrame`` partition in terms of total
 316:         (uncompressed) parquet storage space. This argument is currenlty used to
 317:         set the default value of ``split_row_groups`` (using row-group metadata
 318:         from a single file), and will be ignored if ``split_row_groups`` is not
 319:         set to 'infer' or 'adaptive'. Default may be engine-dependant, but is
 320:         128 MiB for the 'pyarrow' and 'fastparquet' engines.
 321:     aggregate_files : bool or str, default None
 322:         WARNING: The ``aggregate_files`` argument will be deprecated in the future.
 323:         Please consider using ``from_map`` to create a DataFrame collection with a
 324:         custom file-to-partition mapping. If you strongly oppose the deprecation of
 325:         ``aggregate_files``, comment at https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/9051".
 326: 
 327:         Whether distinct file paths may be aggregated into the same output
 328:         partition. This parameter is only used when `split_row_groups` is set to
 329:         'infer', 'adaptive' or to an integer >1. A setting of True means that any
 330:         two file paths may be aggregated into the same output partition, while
 331:         False means that inter-file aggregation is prohibited.
 332: 
 333:         For "hive-partitioned" datasets, a "partition"-column name can also be
 334:         specified. In this case, we allow the aggregation of any two files
 335:         sharing a file path up to, and including, the corresponding directory name.
 336:         For example, if ``aggregate_files`` is set to ``"section"`` for the
 337:         directory structure below, ``03.parquet`` and ``04.parquet`` may be
 338:         aggregated together, but ``01.parquet`` and ``02.parquet`` cannot be.
 339:         If, however, ``aggregate_files`` is set to ``"region"``, ``01.parquet``
 340:         may be aggregated with ``02.parquet``, and ``03.parquet`` may be aggregated
 341:         with ``04.parquet``::
 342: 
 343:             dataset-path/
 344:             ├── region=1/
 345:             │   ├── section=a/
 346:             │   │   └── 01.parquet
 347:             │   ├── section=b/
 348:             │   └── └── 02.parquet
 349:             └── region=2/
 350:                 ├── section=a/
 351:                 │   ├── 03.parquet
 352:                 └── └── 04.parquet
 353: 
 354:         Note that the default behavior of ``aggregate_files`` is ``False``.
 355:     parquet_file_extension: str, tuple[str], or None, default (".parq", ".parquet", ".pq")
 356:         A file extension or an iterable of extensions to use when discovering
 357:         parquet files in a directory. Files that don't match these extensions
 358:         will be ignored. This argument only applies when ``paths`` corresponds
 359:         to a directory and no ``_metadata`` file is present (or
 360:         ``ignore_metadata_file=True``). Passing in ``parquet_file_extension=None``
 361:         will treat all files in the directory as parquet files.
 362: 
 363:         The purpose of this argument is to ensure that the engine will ignore
 364:         unsupported metadata files (like Spark's '_SUCCESS' and 'crc' files).
 365:         It may be necessary to change this argument if the data files in your
 366:         parquet dataset do not end in ".parq", ".parquet", or ".pq".
 367:     filesystem: "fsspec", "arrow", or fsspec.AbstractFileSystem backend to use.
 368:         Note that the "fastparquet" engine only supports "fsspec" or an explicit
 369:         ``pyarrow.fs.AbstractFileSystem`` object. Default is "fsspec".
 370:     dataset: dict, default None
 371:         Dictionary of options to use when creating a ``pyarrow.dataset.Dataset``
 372:         or ``fastparquet.ParquetFile`` object. These options may include a
 373:         "filesystem" key (or "fs" for the "fastparquet" engine) to configure
 374:         the desired file-system backend. However, the top-level ``filesystem``
 375:         argument will always take precedence.
 376: 
 377:         NOTE: For the "pyarrow" engine, the ``dataset`` options may include a
 378:         "partitioning" key. However, since ``pyarrow.dataset.Partitioning``
 379:         objects cannot be serialized, the value can be a dict of key-word
 380:         arguments for the ``pyarrow.dataset.partitioning`` API
 381:         (e.g. ``dataset={"partitioning": {"flavor": "hive", "schema": ...}}``).
 382:         Note that partitioned columns will not be converted to categorical
 383:         dtypes when a custom partitioning schema is specified in this way.
 384:     read: dict, default None
 385:         Dictionary of options to pass through to ``engine.read_partitions``
 386:         using the ``read`` key-word argument.
 387:     arrow_to_pandas: dict, default None
 388:         Dictionary of options to use when converting from ``pyarrow.Table`` to
 389:         a pandas ``DataFrame`` object. Only used by the "arrow" engine.
 390:     **kwargs: dict (of dicts)
 391:         Options to pass through to ``engine.read_partitions`` as stand-alone
 392:         key-word arguments. Note that these options will be ignored by the
 393:         engines defined in ``dask.dataframe``, but may be used by other custom
 394:         implementations.
 395: 
 396:     Examples
 397:     --------
 398:     >>> df = dd.read_parquet('s3://bucket/my-parquet-data')  # doctest: +SKIP
 399: 
 400:     See Also
 401:     --------
 402:     to_parquet
 403:     pyarrow.parquet.ParquetDataset
 404:     """
 405: 
 406:     if use_nullable_dtypes:
 407:         use_nullable_dtypes = dask.config.get("dataframe.dtype_backend")
 408: 
 409:     # Handle `chunksize` deprecation
 410:     if "chunksize" in kwargs:
 411:         if blocksize != "default":
 412:             raise ValueError(
 413:                 "The `chunksize` argument is now deprecated. "
 414:                 "Please use the `blocksize` argument instead."
 415:             )
 416:         blocksize = kwargs.pop("chunksize")
 417:         warnings.warn(
 418:             "The `chunksize` argument is deprecated, and will be "
 419:             "removed in a future release. Setting the `blocksize` "
 420:             "argument instead. Please see documentation on the "
 421:             "`blocksize` argument for more information.",
 422:             FutureWarning,
 423:         )
 424: 
 425:     # "Pre-deprecation" warning for `aggregate_files`
 426:     if aggregate_files:
 427:         warnings.warn(
 428:             "The `aggregate_files` argument will be deprecated in the future. "
 429:             "Please consider using `from_map` to create a DataFrame collection "
 430:             "with a custom file-to-partition mapping.\n\n"
 431:             "If you strongly oppose the deprecation of `aggregate_files`, "
 432:             "please comment at https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/9051",
 433:             FutureWarning,
 434:         )
 435: 
 436:     if "read_from_paths" in kwargs:
 437:         kwargs.pop("read_from_paths")
 438:         warnings.warn(
 439:             "`read_from_paths` is no longer supported and will be ignored.",
 440:             FutureWarning,
 441:         )
 442: 
 443:     # Handle gather_statistics deprecation
 444:     if "gather_statistics" in kwargs:
 445:         if calculate_divisions is None:
 446:             calculate_divisions = kwargs.pop("gather_statistics")
 447:             warnings.warn(
 448:                 "``gather_statistics`` is deprecated and will be removed in a "
 449:                 "future release. Please use ``calculate_divisions`` instead.",
 450:                 FutureWarning,
 451:             )
 452:         else:
 453:             warnings.warn(
 454:                 f"``gather_statistics`` is deprecated. Ignoring this option "
 455:                 f"in favor of ``calculate_divisions={calculate_divisions}``",
 456:                 FutureWarning,
 457:             )
 458:     calculate_divisions = bool(calculate_divisions)
 459: 
 460:     # We support a top-level `parquet_file_extension` kwarg, but
 461:     # must check if the deprecated `require_extension` option is
 462:     # being passed to the engine. If `parquet_file_extension` is
 463:     # set to the default value, and `require_extension` was also
 464:     # specified, we will use `require_extension` but warn the user.
 465:     if (
 466:         "dataset" in kwargs
 467:         and "require_extension" in kwargs["dataset"]
 468:         and parquet_file_extension == (".parq", ".parquet", ".pq")
 469:     ):
 470:         parquet_file_extension = kwargs["dataset"].pop("require_extension")
 471:         warnings.warn(
 472:             "require_extension is deprecated, and will be removed from "
 473:             "read_parquet in a future release. Please use the top-level "
 474:             "parquet_file_extension argument instead.",
 475:             FutureWarning,
 476:         )
 477: 
 478:     # Store initial function arguments
 479:     input_kwargs = {
 480:         "columns": columns,
 481:         "filters": filters,
 482:         "categories": categories,
 483:         "index": index,
 484:         "storage_options": storage_options,
 485:         "engine": engine,
 486:         "use_nullable_dtypes": use_nullable_dtypes,
 487:         "calculate_divisions": calculate_divisions,
 488:         "ignore_metadata_file": ignore_metadata_file,
 489:         "metadata_task_size": metadata_task_size,
 490:         "split_row_groups": split_row_groups,
 491:         "blocksize": blocksize,
 492:         "aggregate_files": aggregate_files,
 493:         "parquet_file_extension": parquet_file_extension,
 494:         "filesystem": filesystem,
 495:         **kwargs,
 496:     }
 497: 
 498:     if isinstance(columns, str):
 499:         input_kwargs["columns"] = [columns]
 500:         df = read_parquet(path, **input_kwargs)
 501:         return df[columns]
 502: 
 503:     if columns is not None:
 504:         columns = list(columns)
 505: 
 506:     if isinstance(engine, str):
 507:         engine = get_engine(engine)
 508: 
 509:     if hasattr(path, "name"):
 510:         path = stringify_path(path)
 511: 
 512:     # Update input_kwargs
 513:     input_kwargs.update({"columns": columns, "engine": engine})
 514: 
 515:     # Process and split user options
 516:     (
 517:         dataset_options,
 518:         read_options,
 519:         open_file_options,
 520:         other_options,
 521:     ) = _split_user_options(**kwargs)
 522: 
 523:     # Extract global filesystem and paths
 524:     fs, paths, dataset_options, open_file_options = engine.extract_filesystem(
 525:         path,
 526:         filesystem,
 527:         dataset_options,
 528:         open_file_options,
 529:         storage_options,
 530:     )
 531:     read_options["open_file_options"] = open_file_options
 532:     paths = sorted(paths, key=natural_sort_key)  # numeric rather than glob ordering
 533: 
 534:     auto_index_allowed = False
 535:     if index is None:
 536:         # User is allowing auto-detected index
 537:         auto_index_allowed = True
 538:     if index and isinstance(index, str):
 539:         index = [index]
 540: 
 541:     if split_row_groups in ("infer", "adaptive"):
 542:         # Using blocksize to plan partitioning
 543:         blocksize = engine.default_blocksize() if blocksize == "default" else blocksize
 544:     else:
 545:         # Not using blocksize - Set to `None`
 546:         blocksize = None
 547: 
 548:     read_metadata_result = engine.read_metadata(
 549:         fs,
 550:         paths,
 551:         categories=categories,
 552:         index=index,
 553:         use_nullable_dtypes=use_nullable_dtypes,
 554:         gather_statistics=calculate_divisions,
 555:         filters=filters,
 556:         split_row_groups=split_row_groups,
 557:         blocksize=blocksize,
 558:         aggregate_files=aggregate_files,
 559:         ignore_metadata_file=ignore_metadata_file,
 560:         metadata_task_size=metadata_task_size,
 561:         parquet_file_extension=parquet_file_extension,
 562:         dataset=dataset_options,
 563:         read=read_options,
 564:         **other_options,
 565:     )
 566: 
 567:     # In the future, we may want to give the engine the
 568:     # option to return a dedicated element for `common_kwargs`.
 569:     # However, to avoid breaking the API, we just embed this
 570:     # data in the first element of `parts` for now.
 571:     # The logic below is inteded to handle backward and forward
 572:     # compatibility with a user-defined engine.
 573:     meta, statistics, parts, index = read_metadata_result[:4]
 574:     common_kwargs = {}
 575:     aggregation_depth = False
 576:     if len(parts):
 577:         # For now, `common_kwargs` and `aggregation_depth`
 578:         # may be stored in the first element of `parts`
 579:         common_kwargs = parts[0].pop("common_kwargs", {})
 580:         aggregation_depth = parts[0].pop("aggregation_depth", aggregation_depth)
 581:         split_row_groups = parts[0].pop("split_row_groups", split_row_groups)
 582: 
 583:     # Parse dataset statistics from metadata (if available)
 584:     parts, divisions, index = process_statistics(
 585:         parts,
 586:         statistics,
 587:         filters,
 588:         index,
 589:         (blocksize if split_row_groups is True else None),
 590:         split_row_groups,
 591:         fs,
 592:         aggregation_depth,
 593:     )
 594: 
 595:     # Account for index and columns arguments.
 596:     # Modify `meta` dataframe accordingly
 597:     meta, index, columns = set_index_columns(meta, index, columns, auto_index_allowed)
 598:     if meta.index.name == NONE_LABEL:
 599:         meta.index.name = None
 600: 
 601:     if len(divisions) < 2:
 602:         # empty dataframe - just use meta
 603:         divisions = (None, None)
 604:         io_func = lambda x: x
 605:         parts = [meta]
 606:     else:
 607:         # Use IO function wrapper
 608:         io_func = ParquetFunctionWrapper(
 609:             engine,
 610:             fs,
 611:             meta,
 612:             columns,
 613:             index,
 614:             use_nullable_dtypes,
 615:             {},  # All kwargs should now be in `common_kwargs`
 616:             common_kwargs,
 617:         )
 618: 
 619:     # If we are using a remote filesystem and retries is not set, bump it
 620:     # to be more fault tolerant, as transient transport errors can occur.
 621:     # The specific number 5 isn't hugely motivated: it's less than ten and more
 622:     # than two.
 623:     annotations = dask.config.get("annotations", {})
 624:     if "retries" not in annotations and not _is_local_fs(fs):
 625:         ctx = dask.annotate(retries=5)
 626:     else:
 627:         ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()  # type: ignore
 628: 
 629:     with ctx:
 630:         # Construct the output collection with from_map
 631:         return from_map(
 632:             io_func,
 633:             parts,
 634:             meta=meta,
 635:             divisions=divisions,
 636:             label="read-parquet",
 637:             token=tokenize(path, **input_kwargs),
 638:             enforce_metadata=False,
 639:             creation_info={
 640:                 "func": read_parquet,
 641:                 "args": (path,),
 642:                 "kwargs": input_kwargs,
 643:             },
 644:         )
 645: 
 646: 
 647: def check_multi_support(engine):
 648:     # Helper function to check that the engine
 649:     # supports a multi-partition read
 650:     return hasattr(engine, "multi_support") and engine.multi_support()
 651: 
 652: 
 653: def read_parquet_part(
 654:     fs, engine, meta, part, columns, index, use_nullable_dtypes, kwargs
 655: ):
 656:     """Read a part of a parquet dataset
 657: 
 658:     This function is used by `read_parquet`."""
 659:     if isinstance(part, list):
 660:         if len(part) == 1 or part[0][1] or not check_multi_support(engine):
 661:             # Part kwargs expected
 662:             func = engine.read_partition
 663:             dfs = [
 664:                 func(
 665:                     fs,
 666:                     rg,
 667:                     columns.copy(),
 668:                     index,
 669:                     use_nullable_dtypes=use_nullable_dtypes,
 670:                     **toolz.merge(kwargs, kw),
 671:                 )
 672:                 for (rg, kw) in part
 673:             ]
 674:             df = concat(dfs, axis=0) if len(dfs) > 1 else dfs[0]
 675:         else:
 676:             # No part specific kwargs, let engine read
 677:             # list of parts at once
 678:             df = engine.read_partition(
 679:                 fs,
 680:                 [p[0] for p in part],
 681:                 columns.copy(),
 682:                 index,
 683:                 use_nullable_dtypes=use_nullable_dtypes,
 684:                 **kwargs,
 685:             )
 686:     else:
 687:         # NOTE: `kwargs` are the same for all parts, while `part_kwargs` may
 688:         #       be different for each part.
 689:         rg, part_kwargs = part
 690:         df = engine.read_partition(
 691:             fs,
 692:             rg,
 693:             columns,
 694:             index,
 695:             use_nullable_dtypes=use_nullable_dtypes,
 696:             **toolz.merge(kwargs, part_kwargs),
 697:         )
 698: 
 699:     if meta.columns.name:
 700:         df.columns.name = meta.columns.name
 701:     columns = columns or []
 702:     index = index or []
 703:     df = df[[c for c in columns if c not in index]]
 704:     if index == [NONE_LABEL]:
 705:         df.index.name = None
 706:     return df
 707: 
 708: 
 709: def to_parquet(
 710:     df,
 711:     path,
 712:     engine="auto",
 713:     compression="snappy",
 714:     write_index=True,
 715:     append=False,
 716:     overwrite=False,
 717:     ignore_divisions=False,
 718:     partition_on=None,
 719:     storage_options=None,
 720:     custom_metadata=None,
 721:     write_metadata_file=None,
 722:     compute=True,
 723:     compute_kwargs=None,
 724:     schema="infer",
 725:     name_function=None,
 726:     filesystem=None,
 727:     **kwargs,
 728: ):
 729:     """Store Dask.dataframe to Parquet files
 730: 
 731:     Notes
 732:     -----
 733:     Each partition will be written to a separate file.
 734: 
 735:     Parameters
 736:     ----------
 737:     df : dask.dataframe.DataFrame
 738:     path : string or pathlib.Path
 739:         Destination directory for data.  Prepend with protocol like ``s3://``
 740:         or ``hdfs://`` for remote data.
 741:     engine : {'auto', 'pyarrow', 'fastparquet'}, default 'auto'
 742:         Parquet library to use. Defaults to 'auto', which uses ``pyarrow`` if
 743:         it is installed, and falls back to ``fastparquet`` otherwise.
 744:     compression : string or dict, default 'snappy'
 745:         Either a string like ``"snappy"`` or a dictionary mapping column names
 746:         to compressors like ``{"name": "gzip", "values": "snappy"}``. Defaults
 747:         to ``"snappy"``.
 748:     write_index : boolean, default True
 749:         Whether or not to write the index. Defaults to True.
 750:     append : bool, default False
 751:         If False (default), construct data-set from scratch. If True, add new
 752:         row-group(s) to an existing data-set. In the latter case, the data-set
 753:         must exist, and the schema must match the input data.
 754:     overwrite : bool, default False
 755:         Whether or not to remove the contents of `path` before writing the dataset.
 756:         The default is False.  If True, the specified path must correspond to
 757:         a directory (but not the current working directory).  This option cannot
 758:         be set to True if `append=True`.
 759:         NOTE: `overwrite=True` will remove the original data even if the current
 760:         write operation fails.  Use at your own risk.
 761:     ignore_divisions : bool, default False
 762:         If False (default) raises error when previous divisions overlap with
 763:         the new appended divisions. Ignored if append=False.
 764:     partition_on : list, default None
 765:         Construct directory-based partitioning by splitting on these fields'
 766:         values. Each dask partition will result in one or more datafiles,
 767:         there will be no global groupby.
 768:     storage_options : dict, default None
 769:         Key/value pairs to be passed on to the file-system backend, if any.
 770:     custom_metadata : dict, default None
 771:         Custom key/value metadata to include in all footer metadata (and
 772:         in the global "_metadata" file, if applicable).  Note that the custom
 773:         metadata may not contain the reserved b"pandas" key.
 774:     write_metadata_file : bool or None, default None
 775:         Whether to write the special ``_metadata`` file. If ``None`` (the
 776:         default), a ``_metadata`` file will only be written if ``append=True``
 777:         and the dataset already has a ``_metadata`` file.
 778:     compute : bool, default True
 779:         If ``True`` (default) then the result is computed immediately. If
 780:         ``False`` then a ``dask.dataframe.Scalar`` object is returned for
 781:         future computation.
 782:     compute_kwargs : dict, default True
 783:         Options to be passed in to the compute method
 784:     schema : pyarrow.Schema, dict, "infer", or None, default "infer"
 785:         Global schema to use for the output dataset. Defaults to "infer", which
 786:         will infer the schema from the dask dataframe metadata. This is usually
 787:         sufficient for common schemas, but notably will fail for ``object``
 788:         dtype columns that contain things other than strings. These columns
 789:         will require an explicit schema be specified. The schema for a subset
 790:         of columns can be overridden by passing in a dict of column names to
 791:         pyarrow types (for example ``schema={"field": pa.string()}``); columns
 792:         not present in this dict will still be automatically inferred.
 793:         Alternatively, a full ``pyarrow.Schema`` may be passed, in which case
 794:         no schema inference will be done. Passing in ``schema=None`` will
 795:         disable the use of a global file schema - each written file may use a
 796:         different schema dependent on the dtypes of the corresponding
 797:         partition. Note that this argument is ignored by the "fastparquet"
 798:         engine.
 799:     name_function : callable, default None
 800:         Function to generate the filename for each output partition.
 801:         The function should accept an integer (partition index) as input and
 802:         return a string which will be used as the filename for the corresponding
 803:         partition. Should preserve the lexicographic order of partitions.
 804:         If not specified, files will created using the convention
 805:         ``part.0.parquet``, ``part.1.parquet``, ``part.2.parquet``, ...
 806:         and so on for each partition in the DataFrame.
 807:     filesystem: "fsspec", "arrow", or fsspec.AbstractFileSystem backend to use.
 808:         Note that the "fastparquet" engine only supports "fsspec" or an explicit
 809:         ``pyarrow.fs.AbstractFileSystem`` object. Default is "fsspec".
 810:     **kwargs :
 811:         Extra options to be passed on to the specific backend.
 812: 
 813:     Examples
 814:     --------
 815:     >>> df = dd.read_csv(...)  # doctest: +SKIP
 816:     >>> df.to_parquet('/path/to/output/', ...)  # doctest: +SKIP
 817: 
 818:     By default, files will be created in the specified output directory using the
 819:     convention ``part.0.parquet``, ``part.1.parquet``, ``part.2.parquet``, ... and so on for
 820:     each partition in the DataFrame. To customize the names of each file, you can use the
 821:     ``name_function=`` keyword argument. The function passed to ``name_function`` will be
 822:     used to generate the filename for each partition and should expect a partition's index
 823:     integer as input and return a string which will be used as the filename for the corresponding
 824:     partition. Strings produced by ``name_function`` must preserve the order of their respective
 825:     partition indices.
 826: 
 827:     For example:
 828: 
 829:     >>> name_function = lambda x: f"data-{x}.parquet"
 830:     >>> df.to_parquet('/path/to/output/', name_function=name_function)  # doctest: +SKIP
 831: 
 832:     will result in the following files being created::
 833: 
 834:         /path/to/output/
 835:             ├── data-0.parquet
 836:             ├── data-1.parquet
 837:             ├── data-2.parquet
 838:             └── ...
 839: 
 840:     See Also
 841:     --------
 842:     read_parquet: Read parquet data to dask.dataframe
 843:     """
 844:     compute_kwargs = compute_kwargs or {}
 845: 
 846:     partition_on = partition_on or []
 847:     if isinstance(partition_on, str):
 848:         partition_on = [partition_on]
 849: 
 850:     if set(partition_on) - set(df.columns):
 851:         raise ValueError(
 852:             "Partitioning on non-existent column. "
 853:             "partition_on=%s ."
 854:             "columns=%s" % (str(partition_on), str(list(df.columns)))
 855:         )
 856: 
 857:     if df.columns.inferred_type not in {"string", "empty"}:
 858:         raise ValueError("parquet doesn't support non-string column names")
 859: 
 860:     if isinstance(engine, str):
 861:         engine = get_engine(engine)
 862: 
 863:     if hasattr(path, "name"):
 864:         path = stringify_path(path)
 865: 
 866:     fs, _paths, _, _ = engine.extract_filesystem(
 867:         path,
 868:         filesystem=filesystem,
 869:         dataset_options={},
 870:         open_file_options={},
 871:         storage_options=storage_options,
 872:     )
 873:     assert len(_paths) == 1, "only one path"
 874:     path = _paths[0]
 875: 
 876:     if overwrite:
 877:         if append:
 878:             raise ValueError("Cannot use both `overwrite=True` and `append=True`!")
 879: 
 880:         if fs.exists(path) and fs.isdir(path):
 881:             # Check for any previous parquet layers reading from a file in the
 882:             # output directory, since deleting those files now would result in
 883:             # errors or incorrect results.
 884:             for layer_name, layer in df.dask.layers.items():
 885:                 if layer_name.startswith("read-parquet-") and isinstance(
 886:                     layer, DataFrameIOLayer
 887:                 ):
 888:                     path_with_slash = path.rstrip("/") + "/"  # ensure trailing slash
 889:                     for input in layer.inputs:
 890:                         # Note that `input` may be either `dict` or `List[dict]`
 891:                         for piece_dict in input if isinstance(input, list) else [input]:
 892:                             if piece_dict["piece"][0].startswith(path_with_slash):
 893:                                 raise ValueError(
 894:                                     "Reading and writing to the same parquet file within "
 895:                                     "the same task graph is not supported."
 896:                                 )
 897: 
 898:             # Don't remove the directory if it's the current working directory
 899:             if _is_local_fs(fs):
 900:                 working_dir = fs.expand_path(".")[0]
 901:                 if path.rstrip("/") == working_dir.rstrip("/"):
 902:                     raise ValueError(
 903:                         "Cannot clear the contents of the current working directory!"
 904:                     )
 905: 
 906:             # It's safe to clear the output directory
 907:             fs.rm(path, recursive=True)
 908: 
 909:     # Always skip divisions checks if divisions are unknown
 910:     if not df.known_divisions:
 911:         ignore_divisions = True
 912: 
 913:     # Save divisions and corresponding index name. This is necessary,
 914:     # because we may be resetting the index to write the file
 915:     division_info = {"divisions": df.divisions, "name": df.index.name}
 916:     if division_info["name"] is None:
 917:         # As of 0.24.2, pandas will rename an index with name=None
 918:         # when df.reset_index() is called.  The default name is "index",
 919:         # but dask will always change the name to the NONE_LABEL constant
 920:         if NONE_LABEL not in df.columns:
 921:             division_info["name"] = NONE_LABEL
 922:         elif write_index:
 923:             raise ValueError(
 924:                 "Index must have a name if __null_dask_index__ is a column."
 925:             )
 926:         else:
 927:             warnings.warn(
 928:                 "If read back by Dask, column named __null_dask_index__ "
 929:                 "will be set to the index (and renamed to None)."
 930:             )
 931: 
 932:     # There are some "reserved" names that may be used as the default column
 933:     # name after resetting the index. However, we don't want to treat it as
 934:     # a "special" name if the string is already used as a "real" column name.
 935:     reserved_names = []
 936:     for name in ["index", "level_0"]:
 937:         if name not in df.columns:
 938:             reserved_names.append(name)
 939: 
 940:     # If write_index==True (default), reset the index and record the
 941:     # name of the original index in `index_cols` (we will set the name
 942:     # to the NONE_LABEL constant if it is originally `None`).
 943:     # `fastparquet` will use `index_cols` to specify the index column(s)
 944:     # in the metadata.  `pyarrow` will revert the `reset_index` call
 945:     # below if `index_cols` is populated (because pyarrow will want to handle
 946:     # index preservation itself).  For both engines, the column index
 947:     # will be written to "pandas metadata" if write_index=True
 948:     index_cols = []
 949:     if write_index:
 950:         real_cols = set(df.columns)
 951:         none_index = list(df._meta.index.names) == [None]
 952:         df = df.reset_index()
 953:         if none_index:
 954:             df.columns = [
 955:                 c if c not in reserved_names else NONE_LABEL for c in df.columns
 956:             ]
 957:         index_cols = [c for c in set(df.columns) - real_cols]
 958:     else:
 959:         # Not writing index - might as well drop it
 960:         df = df.reset_index(drop=True)
 961: 
 962:     if custom_metadata and b"pandas" in custom_metadata.keys():
 963:         raise ValueError(
 964:             "User-defined key/value metadata (custom_metadata) can not "
 965:             "contain a b'pandas' key.  This key is reserved by Pandas, "
 966:             "and overwriting the corresponding value can render the "
 967:             "entire dataset unreadable."
 968:         )
 969: 
 970:     # Engine-specific initialization steps to write the dataset.
 971:     # Possibly create parquet metadata, and load existing stuff if appending
 972:     i_offset, fmd, metadata_file_exists, extra_write_kwargs = engine.initialize_write(
 973:         df,
 974:         fs,
 975:         path,
 976:         append=append,
 977:         ignore_divisions=ignore_divisions,
 978:         partition_on=partition_on,
 979:         division_info=division_info,
 980:         index_cols=index_cols,
 981:         schema=schema,
 982:         custom_metadata=custom_metadata,
 983:         **kwargs,
 984:     )
 985: 
 986:     # By default we only write a metadata file when appending if one already
 987:     # exists
 988:     if append and write_metadata_file is None:
 989:         write_metadata_file = metadata_file_exists
 990: 
 991:     # Check that custom name_function is valid,
 992:     # and that it will produce unique names
 993:     if name_function is not None:
 994:         if not callable(name_function):
 995:             raise ValueError("``name_function`` must be a callable with one argument.")
 996:         filenames = [name_function(i + i_offset) for i in range(df.npartitions)]
 997:         if len(set(filenames)) < len(filenames):
 998:             raise ValueError("``name_function`` must produce unique filenames.")
 999: 
1000:     # If we are using a remote filesystem and retries is not set, bump it
1001:     # to be more fault tolerant, as transient transport errors can occur.
1002:     # The specific number 5 isn't hugely motivated: it's less than ten and more
1003:     # than two.
1004:     annotations = dask.config.get("annotations", {})
1005:     if "retries" not in annotations and not _is_local_fs(fs):
1006:         ctx = dask.annotate(retries=5)
1007:     else:
1008:         ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()
1009: 
1010:     # Create Blockwise layer for parquet-data write
1011:     with ctx:
1012:         data_write = df.map_partitions(
1013:             ToParquetFunctionWrapper(
1014:                 engine,
1015:                 path,
1016:                 fs,
1017:                 partition_on,
1018:                 write_metadata_file,
1019:                 i_offset,
1020:                 name_function,
1021:                 toolz.merge(
1022:                     kwargs,
1023:                     {"compression": compression, "custom_metadata": custom_metadata},
1024:                     extra_write_kwargs,
1025:                 ),
1026:             ),
1027:             BlockIndex((df.npartitions,)),
1028:             # Pass in the original metadata to avoid
1029:             # metadata emulation in `map_partitions`.
1030:             # This is necessary, because we are not
1031:             # expecting a dataframe-like output.
1032:             meta=df._meta,
1033:             enforce_metadata=False,
1034:             transform_divisions=False,
1035:             align_dataframes=False,
1036:         )
1037: 
1038:     # Collect metadata and write _metadata.
1039:     # TODO: Use tree-reduction layer (when available)
1040:     if write_metadata_file:
1041:         final_name = "metadata-" + data_write._name
1042:         dsk = {
1043:             (final_name, 0): (
1044:                 apply,
1045:                 engine.write_metadata,
1046:                 [
1047:                     data_write.__dask_keys__(),
1048:                     fmd,
1049:                     fs,
1050:                     path,
1051:                 ],
1052:                 {"append": append, "compression": compression},
1053:             )
1054:         }
1055:     else:
1056:         # NOTE: We still define a single task to tie everything together
1057:         # when we are not writing a _metadata file. We do not want to
1058:         # return `data_write` (or a `data_write.to_bag()`), because calling
1059:         # `compute()` on a multi-partition collection requires the overhead
1060:         # of trying to concatenate results on the client.
1061:         final_name = "store-" + data_write._name
1062:         dsk = {(final_name, 0): (lambda x: None, data_write.__dask_keys__())}
1063: 
1064:     # Convert data_write + dsk to computable collection
1065:     graph = HighLevelGraph.from_collections(final_name, dsk, dependencies=(data_write,))
1066:     out = Scalar(graph, final_name, "")
1067: 
1068:     if compute:
1069:         out = out.compute(**compute_kwargs)
1070: 
1071:     # Invalidate the filesystem listing cache for the output path after write.
1072:     # We do this before returning, even if `compute=False`. This helps ensure
1073:     # that reading files that were just written succeeds.
1074:     fs.invalidate_cache(path)
1075: 
1076:     return out
1077: 
1078: 
1079: def create_metadata_file(
1080:     paths,
1081:     root_dir=None,
1082:     out_dir=None,
1083:     engine="pyarrow",
1084:     storage_options=None,
1085:     split_every=32,
1086:     compute=True,
1087:     compute_kwargs=None,
1088:     fs=None,
1089: ):
1090:     """Construct a global _metadata file from a list of parquet files.
1091: 
1092:     Dask's read_parquet function is designed to leverage a global
1093:     _metadata file whenever one is available.  The to_parquet
1094:     function will generate this file automatically by default, but it
1095:     may not exist if the dataset was generated outside of Dask.  This
1096:     utility provides a mechanism to generate a _metadata file from a
1097:     list of existing parquet files.
1098: 
1099:     NOTE: This utility is not yet supported for the "fastparquet" engine.
1100: 
1101:     Parameters
1102:     ----------
1103:     paths : list(string)
1104:         List of files to collect footer metadata from.
1105:     root_dir : string, optional
1106:         Root directory of dataset.  The `file_path` fields in the new
1107:         _metadata file will relative to this directory.  If None, a common
1108:         root directory will be inferred.
1109:     out_dir : string or False, optional
1110:         Directory location to write the final _metadata file.  By default,
1111:         this will be set to `root_dir`.  If False is specified, the global
1112:         metadata will be returned as an in-memory object (and will not be
1113:         written to disk).
1114:     engine : str or Engine, default 'pyarrow'
1115:         Parquet Engine to use. Only 'pyarrow' is supported if a string
1116:         is passed.
1117:     storage_options : dict, optional
1118:         Key/value pairs to be passed on to the file-system backend, if any.
1119:     split_every : int, optional
1120:         The final metadata object that is written to _metadata can be much
1121:         smaller than the list of footer metadata. In order to avoid the
1122:         aggregation of all metadata within a single task, a tree reduction
1123:         is used.  This argument specifies the maximum number of metadata
1124:         inputs to be handled by any one task in the tree. Defaults to 32.
1125:     compute : bool, optional
1126:         If True (default) then the result is computed immediately. If False
1127:         then a ``dask.delayed`` object is returned for future computation.
1128:     compute_kwargs : dict, optional
1129:         Options to be passed in to the compute method
1130:     fs : fsspec object, optional
1131:         File-system instance to use for file handling. If prefixes have
1132:         been removed from the elements of ``paths`` before calling this
1133:         function, an ``fs`` argument must be provided to ensure correct
1134:         behavior on remote file systems ("naked" paths cannot be used
1135:         to infer file-system information).
1136:     """
1137: 
1138:     # Get engine.
1139:     # Note that "fastparquet" is not yet supported
1140:     if isinstance(engine, str):
1141:         if engine not in ("pyarrow", "arrow"):
1142:             raise ValueError(
1143:                 f"{engine} is not a supported engine for create_metadata_file "
1144:                 "Try engine='pyarrow'."
1145:             )
1146:         engine = get_engine(engine)
1147: 
1148:     # Process input path list
1149:     if fs is None:
1150:         # Only do this if an fsspec file-system object is not
1151:         # already defined. The prefixes may already be stripped.
1152:         fs, _, paths = get_fs_token_paths(
1153:             paths, mode="rb", storage_options=storage_options
1154:         )
1155:     ap_kwargs = {"root": root_dir} if root_dir else {}
1156:     paths, root_dir, fns = _sort_and_analyze_paths(paths, fs, **ap_kwargs)
1157:     out_dir = root_dir if out_dir is None else out_dir
1158: 
1159:     # Start constructing a raw graph
1160:     dsk = {}
1161:     name = "gen-metadata-" + tokenize(paths, fs)
1162:     collect_name = "collect-" + name
1163:     agg_name = "agg-" + name
1164: 
1165:     # Define a "collect" task for each file in the input list.
1166:     # Each tasks will:
1167:     #   1. Extract the footer metadata from a distinct file
1168:     #   2. Populate the `file_path` field in the metadata
1169:     #   3. Return the extracted/modified metadata
1170:     for p, (fn, path) in enumerate(zip(fns, paths)):
1171:         key = (collect_name, p, 0)
1172:         dsk[key] = (engine.collect_file_metadata, path, fs, fn)
1173: 
1174:     # Build a reduction tree to aggregate all footer metadata
1175:     # into a single metadata object.  Each task in the tree
1176:     # will take in a list of metadata objects as input, and will
1177:     # usually output a single (aggregated) metadata object.
1178:     # The final task in the tree will write the result to disk
1179:     # instead of returning it (this behavior is triggered by
1180:     # passing a file path to `engine.aggregate_metadata`).
1181:     parts = len(paths)
1182:     widths = [parts]
1183:     while parts > 1:
1184:         parts = math.ceil(parts / split_every)
1185:         widths.append(parts)
1186:     height = len(widths)
1187:     for depth in range(1, height):
1188:         for group in range(widths[depth]):
1189:             p_max = widths[depth - 1]
1190:             lstart = split_every * group
1191:             lstop = min(lstart + split_every, p_max)
1192:             dep_task_name = collect_name if depth == 1 else agg_name
1193:             node_list = [(dep_task_name, p, depth - 1) for p in range(lstart, lstop)]
1194:             if depth == height - 1:
1195:                 assert group == 0
1196:                 dsk[name] = (engine.aggregate_metadata, node_list, fs, out_dir)
1197:             else:
1198:                 dsk[(agg_name, group, depth)] = (
1199:                     engine.aggregate_metadata,
1200:                     node_list,
1201:                     None,
1202:                     None,
1203:                 )
1204: 
1205:     # There will be no aggregation tasks if there is only one file
1206:     if len(paths) == 1:
1207:         dsk[name] = (engine.aggregate_metadata, [(collect_name, 0, 0)], fs, out_dir)
1208: 
1209:     # Convert the raw graph to a `Delayed` object
1210:     graph = HighLevelGraph.from_collections(name, dsk, dependencies=[])
1211:     out = Delayed(name, graph)
1212: 
1213:     # Optionally compute the result
1214:     if compute:
1215:         if compute_kwargs is None:
1216:             compute_kwargs = dict()
1217:         out = out.compute(**compute_kwargs)
1218:     return out
1219: 
1220: 
1221: _ENGINES: dict[str, Engine] = {}
1222: 
1223: 
1224: def get_engine(engine):
1225:     """Get the parquet engine backend implementation.
1226: 
1227:     Parameters
1228:     ----------
1229:     engine : {'auto', 'pyarrow', 'fastparquet'}, default 'auto'
1230:         Parquet library to use. Defaults to 'auto', which uses ``pyarrow`` if
1231:         it is installed, and falls back to ``fastparquet`` otherwise.
1232: 
1233:     Returns
1234:     -------
1235:     A dict containing a ``'read'`` and ``'write'`` function.
1236:     """
1237:     if engine in _ENGINES:
1238:         return _ENGINES[engine]
1239: 
1240:     if engine == "auto":
1241:         try:
1242:             return get_engine("pyarrow")
1243:         except RuntimeError:
1244:             pass
1245: 
1246:         try:
1247:             return get_engine("fastparquet")
1248:         except RuntimeError:
1249:             raise RuntimeError("Please install either pyarrow or fastparquet") from None
1250: 
1251:     elif engine == "fastparquet":
1252:         import_required("fastparquet", "`fastparquet` not installed")
1253:         from dask.dataframe.io.parquet.fastparquet import FastParquetEngine
1254: 
1255:         _ENGINES["fastparquet"] = eng = FastParquetEngine
1256:         return eng
1257: 
1258:     elif engine in ("pyarrow", "arrow", "pyarrow-dataset"):
1259:         import_required("pyarrow", "`pyarrow` not installed")
1260: 
1261:         if engine in ("pyarrow-dataset", "arrow"):
1262:             engine = "pyarrow"
1263: 
1264:         if engine == "pyarrow":
1265:             from dask.dataframe.io.parquet.arrow import ArrowDatasetEngine
1266: 
1267:             _ENGINES[engine] = eng = ArrowDatasetEngine
1268:         return eng
1269: 
1270:     else:
1271:         raise ValueError(
1272:             f'Unsupported engine: "{engine}".'
1273:             '  Valid choices include "pyarrow" and "fastparquet".'
1274:         )
1275: 
1276: 
1277: #####################
1278: # Utility Functions #
1279: #####################
1280: 
1281: 
1282: def sorted_columns(statistics, columns=None):
1283:     """Find sorted columns given row-group statistics
1284: 
1285:     This finds all columns that are sorted, along with the
1286:     appropriate ``divisions`` for those columns. If the (optional)
1287:     ``columns`` argument is used, the search will be restricted
1288:     to the specified column set.
1289: 
1290:     Returns
1291:     -------
1292:     out: List of {'name': str, 'divisions': List[str]} dictionaries
1293:     """
1294:     if not statistics:
1295:         return []
1296: 
1297:     out = []
1298:     for i, c in enumerate(statistics[0]["columns"]):
1299:         if columns and c["name"] not in columns:
1300:             continue
1301:         if not all(
1302:             "min" in s["columns"][i] and "max" in s["columns"][i] for s in statistics
1303:         ):
1304:             continue
1305:         divisions = [c["min"]]
1306:         max = c["max"]
1307:         success = c["min"] is not None
1308:         for stats in statistics[1:]:
1309:             c = stats["columns"][i]
1310:             if c["min"] is None:
1311:                 success = False
1312:                 break
1313:             if c["min"] >= max:
1314:                 divisions.append(c["min"])
1315:                 max = c["max"]
1316:             else:
1317:                 success = False
1318:                 break
1319: 
1320:         if success:
1321:             divisions.append(max)
1322:             assert divisions == sorted(divisions)
1323:             out.append({"name": c["name"], "divisions": divisions})
1324: 
1325:     return out
1326: 
1327: 
1328: def apply_filters(parts, statistics, filters):
1329:     """Apply filters onto parts/statistics pairs
1330: 
1331:     Parameters
1332:     ----------
1333:     parts: list
1334:         Tokens corresponding to row groups to read in the future
1335:     statistics: List[dict]
1336:         List of statistics for each part, including min and max values
1337:     filters: Union[List[Tuple[str, str, Any]], List[List[Tuple[str, str, Any]]]]
1338:         List of filters to apply, like ``[[('x', '=', 0), ...], ...]``. This
1339:         implements partition-level (hive) filtering only, i.e., to prevent the
1340:         loading of some row-groups and/or files.
1341: 
1342:         Predicates can be expressed in disjunctive normal form (DNF). This means
1343:         that the innermost tuple describes a single column predicate. These
1344:         inner predicates are combined with an AND conjunction into a larger
1345:         predicate. The outer-most list then combines all of the combined
1346:         filters with an OR disjunction.
1347: 
1348:         Predicates can also be expressed as a List[Tuple]. These are evaluated
1349:         as an AND conjunction. To express OR in predicates, one must use the
1350:         (preferred) List[List[Tuple]] notation.
1351: 
1352:         Note that the "fastparquet" engine does not currently support DNF for
1353:         the filtering of partitioned columns (List[Tuple] is required).
1354:     Returns
1355:     -------
1356:     parts, statistics: the same as the input, but possibly a subset
1357:     """
1358: 
1359:     def apply_conjunction(parts, statistics, conjunction):
1360:         for column, operator, value in conjunction:
1361:             if operator == "in" and not isinstance(value, (list, set, tuple)):
1362:                 raise TypeError("Value of 'in' filter must be a list, set, or tuple.")
1363:             out_parts = []
1364:             out_statistics = []
1365:             for part, stats in zip(parts, statistics):
1366:                 if "filter" in stats and stats["filter"]:
1367:                     continue  # Filtered by engine
1368:                 try:
1369:                     c = toolz.groupby("name", stats["columns"])[column][0]
1370:                     min = c["min"]
1371:                     max = c["max"]
1372:                     null_count = c.get("null_count", None)
1373:                 except KeyError:
1374:                     out_parts.append(part)
1375:                     out_statistics.append(stats)
1376:                 else:
1377:                     if (
1378:                         operator != "is not"
1379:                         and min is None
1380:                         and max is None
1381:                         and null_count
1382:                         or operator == "is"
1383:                         and null_count
1384:                         or operator == "is not"
1385:                         and (not pd.isna(min) or not pd.isna(max))
1386:                         or operator in ("==", "=")
1387:                         and min <= value <= max
1388:                         or operator == "!="
1389:                         and (null_count or min != value or max != value)
1390:                         or operator == "<"
1391:                         and min < value
1392:                         or operator == "<="
1393:                         and min <= value
1394:                         or operator == ">"
1395:                         and max > value
1396:                         or operator == ">="
1397:                         and max >= value
1398:                         or operator == "in"
1399:                         and any(min <= item <= max for item in value)
1400:                     ):
1401:                         out_parts.append(part)
1402:                         out_statistics.append(stats)
1403: 
1404:             parts, statistics = out_parts, out_statistics
1405: 
1406:         return parts, statistics
1407: 
1408:     conjunction, *disjunction = filters if isinstance(filters[0], list) else [filters]
1409: 
1410:     out_parts, out_statistics = apply_conjunction(parts, statistics, conjunction)
1411:     for conjunction in disjunction:
1412:         for part, stats in zip(*apply_conjunction(parts, statistics, conjunction)):
1413:             if part not in out_parts:
1414:                 out_parts.append(part)
1415:                 out_statistics.append(stats)
1416: 
1417:     return out_parts, out_statistics
1418: 
1419: 
1420: def process_statistics(
1421:     parts,
1422:     statistics,
1423:     filters,
1424:     index,
1425:     blocksize,
1426:     split_row_groups,
1427:     fs,
1428:     aggregation_depth,
1429: ):
1430:     """Process row-group column statistics in metadata
1431:     Used in read_parquet.
1432:     """
1433:     if statistics and len(parts) != len(statistics):
1434:         # It is up to the Engine to guarantee that these
1435:         # lists are the same length (if statistics are defined).
1436:         # This misalignment may be indicative of a bug or
1437:         # incorrect read_parquet usage, so throw a warning.
1438:         warnings.warn(
1439:             f"Length of partition statistics ({len(statistics)}) "
1440:             f"does not match the partition count ({len(parts)}). "
1441:             f"This may indicate a bug or incorrect read_parquet "
1442:             f"usage. We must ignore the statistics and disable: "
1443:             f"filtering, divisions, and/or file aggregation."
1444:         )
1445:         statistics = []
1446: 
1447:     divisions = None
1448:     if statistics:
1449:         result = list(
1450:             zip(
1451:                 *[
1452:                     (part, stats)
1453:                     for part, stats in zip(parts, statistics)
1454:                     if stats["num-rows"] > 0
1455:                 ]
1456:             )
1457:         )
1458:         parts, statistics = result or [[], []]
1459:         if filters:
1460:             parts, statistics = apply_filters(parts, statistics, filters)
1461: 
1462:         # Aggregate parts/statistics if we are splitting by row-group
1463:         if blocksize or (split_row_groups and int(split_row_groups) > 1):

1482:                 statistics, columns=process_columns
1483:             ):

1486:                     break
1487:                 else:

1489:                     sorted_col_names.append(sorted_column_info["name"])

1504: def set_index_columns(meta, index, columns, auto_index_allowed):
1505:     """Handle index/column arguments, and modify `meta`
1506:     Used in read_parquet.
1507:     """
1508:     ignore_index_column_intersection = False
1509:     if columns is None:
1510:         # User didn't specify columns, so ignore any intersection
1511:         # of auto-detected values with the index (if necessary)
1512:         ignore_index_column_intersection = True
1513:         # Do not allow "un-named" fields to be read in as columns.
1514:         # These were intended to be un-named indices at write time.
1515:         _index = index or []
1516:         columns = [
1517:             c for c in meta.columns if c not in (None, NONE_LABEL) or c in _index
1518:         ]
1519: 
1520:     if not set(columns).issubset(set(meta.columns)):
1521:         raise ValueError(
1522:             "The following columns were not found in the dataset %s\n"
1523:             "The following columns were found %s"
1524:             % (set(columns) - set(meta.columns), meta.columns)
1525:         )
1526: 
1527:     if index:
1528:         if isinstance(index, str):
1529:             index = [index]
1530:         if isinstance(columns, str):
1531:             columns = [columns]
1532: 
1533:         if ignore_index_column_intersection:
1534:             columns = [col for col in columns if col not in index]
1535:         if set(index).intersection(columns):
1536:             if auto_index_allowed:
1537:                 raise ValueError(
1538:                     "Specified index and column arguments must not intersect"
1539:                     " (set index=False or remove the detected index from columns).\n"
1540:                     "index: {} | column: {}".format(index, columns)
1541:                 )
1542:             else:
1543:                 raise ValueError(
1544:                     "Specified index and column arguments must not intersect.\n"
1545:                     "index: {} | column: {}".format(index, columns)
1546:                 )
1547: 
1548:     return meta[list(columns)], index, columns

1551: def aggregate_row_groups(
1552:     parts, stats, blocksize, split_row_groups, fs, aggregation_depth
1553: ):
1554:     if not stats or not stats[0].get("file_path_0", None):
1555:         return parts, stats
1556: 
1557:     parts_agg = []
1558:     stats_agg = []
1559: 
1560:     use_row_group_criteria = split_row_groups and int(split_row_groups) > 1
1561:     use_blocksize_criteria = bool(blocksize)
1562:     if use_blocksize_criteria:
1563:         blocksize = parse_bytes(blocksize)
1564:     next_part, next_stat = [parts[0].copy()], stats[0].copy()
1565:     for i in range(1, len(parts)):
1566:         stat, part = stats[i], parts[i]
1567: 
1568:         # Criteria #1 for aggregating parts: parts are within the same file
1569:         same_path = stat["file_path_0"] == next_stat["file_path_0"]
1570:         multi_path_allowed = False
1571: 
1572:         if aggregation_depth:
1573:             # Criteria #2 for aggregating parts: The part does not include
1574:             # row-group information, or both parts include the same kind
1575:             # of row_group aggregation (all None, or all indices)
1576:             multi_path_allowed = len(part["piece"]) == 1
1577:             if not (same_path or multi_path_allowed):
1578:                 rgs = set(list(part["piece"][1]) + list(next_part[-1]["piece"][1]))
1579:                 multi_path_allowed = (rgs == {None}) or (None not in rgs)
1580: 
1581:             # Criteria #3 for aggregating parts: The parts share a
1582:             # directory at the "depth" allowed by `aggregation_depth`
1583:             if not same_path and multi_path_allowed:
1584:                 if aggregation_depth is True:
1585:                     multi_path_allowed = True
1586:                 elif isinstance(aggregation_depth, int):
1587:                     # Make sure files share the same directory
1588:                     root = stat["file_path_0"].split(fs.sep)[:-aggregation_depth]
1589:                     next_root = next_stat["file_path_0"].split(fs.sep)[
1590:                         :-aggregation_depth
1591:                     ]
1592:                     multi_path_allowed = root == next_root
1593:                 else:
1594:                     raise ValueError(
1595:                         f"{aggregation_depth} not supported for `aggregation_depth`"
1596:                     )
1597: 
1598:         def _check_row_group_criteria(stat, next_stat):
1599:             if use_row_group_criteria:
1600:                 return (next_stat["num-row-groups"] + stat["num-row-groups"]) <= int(
1601:                     split_row_groups
1602:                 )
1603:             else:
1604:                 return False
1605: 
1606:         def _check_blocksize_criteria(stat, next_stat):
1607:             if use_blocksize_criteria:
1608:                 return (
1609:                     next_stat["total_byte_size"] + stat["total_byte_size"]
1610:                 ) <= blocksize
1611:             else:
1612:                 return False
1613: 
1614:         stat["num-row-groups"] = stat.get("num-row-groups", 1)
1615:         next_stat["num-row-groups"] = next_stat.get("num-row-groups", 1)
1616: 
1617:         if (same_path or multi_path_allowed) and (
1618:             _check_row_group_criteria(stat, next_stat)
1619:             or _check_blocksize_criteria(stat, next_stat)
1620:         ):
1621:             # Update part list
1622:             next_piece = next_part[-1]["piece"]
1623:             this_piece = part["piece"]
1624:             if (
1625:                 same_path
1626:                 and len(next_piece) > 1
1627:                 and next_piece[1] != [None]
1628:                 and this_piece[1] != [None]
1629:             ):
1630:                 next_piece[1].extend(this_piece[1])
1631:             else:
1632:                 next_part.append(part)
1633: 
1634:             # Update Statistics
1635:             next_stat["total_byte_size"] += stat["total_byte_size"]
1636:             next_stat["num-rows"] += stat["num-rows"]
1637:             next_stat["num-row-groups"] += stat["num-row-groups"]
1638:             for col, col_add in zip(next_stat["columns"], stat["columns"]):
1639:                 if col["name"] != col_add["name"]:
1640:                     raise ValueError("Columns are different!!")
1641:                 if "min" in col:
1642:                     col["min"] = min(col["min"], col_add["min"])
1643:                 if "max" in col:
1644:                     col["max"] = max(col["max"], col_add["max"])
1645:         else:
1646:             parts_agg.append(next_part)
1647:             stats_agg.append(next_stat)
1648:             next_part, next_stat = [part.copy()], stat.copy()
1649: 
1650:     parts_agg.append(next_part)
1651:     stats_agg.append(next_stat)
1652: 
1653:     return parts_agg, stats_agg
1654:

  72: def shard_df_on_index(df, divisions):
  73:     """Shard a DataFrame by ranges on its index
  74: 
  75:     Examples
  76:     --------
  77: 
  78:     >>> df = pd.DataFrame({'a': [0, 10, 20, 30, 40], 'b': [5, 4 ,3, 2, 1]})
  79:     >>> df
  80:         a  b
  81:     0   0  5
  82:     1  10  4
  83:     2  20  3
  84:     3  30  2
  85:     4  40  1
  86: 
  87:     >>> shards = list(shard_df_on_index(df, [2, 4]))
  88:     >>> shards[0]
  89:         a  b
  90:     0   0  5
  91:     1  10  4
  92: 
  93:     >>> shards[1]
  94:         a  b
  95:     2  20  3
  96:     3  30  2
  97: 
  98:     >>> shards[2]
  99:         a  b
 100:     4  40  1
 101: 
 102:     >>> list(shard_df_on_index(df, []))[0]  # empty case
 103:         a  b
 104:     0   0  5
 105:     1  10  4
 106:     2  20  3
 107:     3  30  2
 108:     4  40  1
 109:     """
 110: 
 111:     if isinstance(divisions, Iterator):
 112:         divisions = list(divisions)
 113:     if not len(divisions):
 114:         yield df
 115:     else:
 116:         divisions = np.array(divisions)
 117:         df = df.sort_index()
 118:         index = df.index
 119:         if is_categorical_dtype(index):
 120:             index = index.as_ordered()
 121:         indices = index.searchsorted(divisions)
 122:         yield df.iloc[: indices[0]]
 123:         for i in range(len(indices) - 1):
 124:             yield df.iloc[indices[i] : indices[i + 1]]
 125:         yield df.iloc[indices[-1] :]

 128: _META_TYPES = "meta : pd.DataFrame, pd.Series, dict, iterable, tuple, optional"
 129: _META_DESCRIPTION = """\
 130: An empty ``pd.DataFrame`` or ``pd.Series`` that matches the dtypes and
 131: column names of the output. This metadata is necessary for many algorithms
 132: in dask dataframe to work.  For ease of use, some alternative inputs are
 133: also available. Instead of a ``DataFrame``, a ``dict`` of ``{name: dtype}``
 134: or iterable of ``(name, dtype)`` can be provided (note that the order of
 135: the names should match the order of the columns). Instead of a series, a
 136: tuple of ``(name, dtype)`` can be used. If not provided, dask will try to
 137: infer the metadata. This may lead to unexpected results, so providing
 138: ``meta`` is recommended. For more information, see
 139: ``dask.dataframe.utils.make_meta``.
 140: """
 141: 
 142: T = TypeVar("T", bound=Callable)
 143: 
 144: 
 145: @overload
 146: def insert_meta_param_description(func: T) -> T:
 147:     ...
 148: 
 149: 
 150: @overload
 151: def insert_meta_param_description(pad: int) -> Callable[[T], T]:
 152:     ...
 153: 
 154: 
 155: def insert_meta_param_description(*args, **kwargs):
 156:     """Replace `$META` in docstring with param description.
 157: 
 158:     If pad keyword is provided, will pad description by that number of
 159:     spaces (default is 8)."""
 160:     if not args:
 161:         return lambda f: insert_meta_param_description(f, **kwargs)
 162:     f = args[0]
 163:     indent = " " * kwargs.get("pad", 8)
 164:     body = textwrap.wrap(
 165:         _META_DESCRIPTION, initial_indent=indent, subsequent_indent=indent, width=78
 166:     )
 167:     descr = "{}\n{}".format(_META_TYPES, "\n".join(body))
 168:     if f.__doc__:
 169:         if "$META" in f.__doc__:
 170:             f.__doc__ = f.__doc__.replace("$META", descr)
 171:         else:
 172:             # Put it at the end of the parameters section
 173:             parameter_header = "Parameters\n%s----------" % indent[4:]
 174:             first, last = re.split("Parameters\\n[ ]*----------", f.__doc__)
 175:             parameters, rest = last.split("\n\n", 1)
 176:             f.__doc__ = "{}{}{}\n{}{}\n\n{}".format(
 177:                 first, parameter_header, parameters, indent[4:], descr, rest
 178:             )
 179:     return f

 196:         tb = "".join(traceback.format_tb(exc_traceback))

 201:                 "determine the type of output that that function returns. \n\n"
 202:                 "To resolve this please provide a meta= keyword.\n"

 220: def has_known_categories(x):
 221:     """Returns whether the categories in `x` are known.
 222: 
 223:     Parameters
 224:     ----------
 225:     x : Series or CategoricalIndex
 226:     """
 227:     x = getattr(x, "_meta", x)
 228:     if is_series_like(x):
 229:         return UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES not in x.cat.categories
 230:     elif is_index_like(x) and hasattr(x, "categories"):
 231:         return UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES not in x.categories
 232:     raise TypeError("Expected Series or CategoricalIndex")

 235: def strip_unknown_categories(x, just_drop_unknown=False):
 236:     """Replace any unknown categoricals with empty categoricals.
 237: 
 238:     Useful for preventing ``UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES`` from leaking into results.
 239:     """
 240:     if isinstance(x, (pd.Series, pd.DataFrame)):
 241:         x = x.copy()
 242:         if isinstance(x, pd.DataFrame):
 243:             cat_mask = x.dtypes == "category"
 244:             if cat_mask.any():
 245:                 cats = cat_mask[cat_mask].index
 246:                 for c in cats:
 247:                     if not has_known_categories(x[c]):
 248:                         if just_drop_unknown:
 249:                             x[c].cat.remove_categories(UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES, inplace=True)
 250:                         else:
 251:                             x[c] = x[c].cat.set_categories([])
 252:         elif isinstance(x, pd.Series):
 253:             if is_categorical_dtype(x.dtype) and not has_known_categories(x):
 254:                 x = x.cat.set_categories([])
 255:         if isinstance(x.index, pd.CategoricalIndex) and not has_known_categories(
 256:             x.index
 257:         ):
 258:             x.index = x.index.set_categories([])
 259:     elif isinstance(x, pd.CategoricalIndex) and not has_known_categories(x):
 260:         x = x.set_categories([])
 261:     return x

 264: def clear_known_categories(x, cols=None, index=True):
 265:     """Set categories to be unknown.
 266: 
 267:     Parameters
 268:     ----------
 269:     x : DataFrame, Series, Index
 270:     cols : iterable, optional
 271:         If x is a DataFrame, set only categoricals in these columns to unknown.
 272:         By default, all categorical columns are set to unknown categoricals
 273:     index : bool, optional
 274:         If True and x is a Series or DataFrame, set the clear known categories
 275:         in the index as well.
 276:     """
 277:     if isinstance(x, (pd.Series, pd.DataFrame)):
 278:         x = x.copy()
 279:         if isinstance(x, pd.DataFrame):
 280:             mask = x.dtypes == "category"
 281:             if cols is None:
 282:                 cols = mask[mask].index
 283:             elif not mask.loc[cols].all():
 284:                 raise ValueError("Not all columns are categoricals")
 285:             for c in cols:
 286:                 x[c] = x[c].cat.set_categories([UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES])
 287:         elif isinstance(x, pd.Series):
 288:             if is_categorical_dtype(x.dtype):
 289:                 x = x.cat.set_categories([UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES])
 290:         if index and isinstance(x.index, pd.CategoricalIndex):
 291:             x.index = x.index.set_categories([UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES])
 292:     elif isinstance(x, pd.CategoricalIndex):
 293:         x = x.set_categories([UNKNOWN_CATEGORIES])

 318: def _scalar_from_dtype(dtype):
 319:     if dtype.kind in ("i", "f", "u"):
 320:         return dtype.type(1)
 321:     elif dtype.kind == "c":
 322:         return dtype.type(complex(1, 0))
 323:     elif dtype.kind in _simple_fake_mapping:
 324:         o = _simple_fake_mapping[dtype.kind]

 326:     else:
